content.content:
1	# AGENTS.md — Designflow Backend: Primary Developer Guide
2	
3	Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. What this service is
8	
9	Designflow Backend is the main REST API for the Designflow PLM platform. It is a stateless Node.js + Express service backed by PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM). Identity comes from a JWT on every request; session state does not exist. The service runs on Google Cloud Run and is reached exclusively through the BFF (`designflow-bff`), which terminates the user JWT and adds an OIDC service-to-service token before forwarding. The frontend is a separate Angular SPA in a sibling repository. For a full system diagram see `docs/architecture.md`.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 2. Multi-model note
14	
15	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
16	
17	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
18	
19	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the "What to ignore" section below.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 3. Repository structure
24	
25	| Path | What it is |
26	|---|---|
27	| `index.js` | Server entry — middleware stack (including the `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` swap) and `listen` |
28	| `config/env.js` | dotenv loader; resolves `.env.${NODE_ENV}` |
29	| `config/db.config.js` | PostgreSQL connection params; throws on missing required key |
30	| `config/digitalocean.config.js` | DigitalOcean Spaces credentials |
31	| `config/brevo.config.js` | Brevo email credentials |
32	| `helpers/auth.js` | JWT RS256 verify; `authRole(roles[])` middleware factory; `getUserInfo(token)` |
33	| `helpers/context.middleware.js` | `AsyncLocalStorage` per-request store `{ user, reqId }` — lets deep-stack code read the current user without prop-drilling |
34	| `helpers/openrouter.js` | OpenRouter API client; prompt-caching markers for anthropic/deepseek/google models |
35	| `helpers/deepseek.js` | DeepSeek direct API client (`api.deepseek.com`); maps `reasoning_effort: 'none'\|'high'\|'max'` to the DeepSeek `thinking` parameter; used when `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set and `api='deepseek-direct'` |
36	| `helpers/section301.js` | Section 301 PDF text extraction + regex HTS parser (lazy-requires `pdf-parse`) |
37	| `helpers/hts-rates.js` | MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC HTS JSON (`data/hts-rates.json`) |
38	| `helpers/trump2025.js` | Trump 2025 reciprocal-tariff lookup; alias-aware country normalization |
39	| `helpers/factories.js` | Factory list / country map / `updateCountry(id, country)` |
40	| `helpers/digitalocean.js` | S3 client; presigned URL, ACL, upload, delete |
41	| `helpers/mention.js` | `parseMentions` / `resolveMentions` for @mention pipeline |
42	| `helpers/multer.js` | Multer instances (memory storage) configured per upload type |
43	| `helpers/errorHandler.js` | Express error-handler middleware |
44	| `helpers/quote-access.js` | `vendorMayModifyQuote(userInfo, rfqVendor)` — ownership guard preventing a vendor from mutating another factory's quote by `quote_id` (see §11) |
45	| `helpers/utility.js` | Shared utility functions |
46	| `models/db.js` | Sequelize init + model registration + **all schema migrations** (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`) |
47	| `models/db/` | 60+ Sequelize model files (one per table) |
48	| `models/lib.model.js` | Legacy domain-method module (`Customer` namespace — see §11) |
49	| `routes/index.js` | Root router — mounts all subrouters |
50	| `routes/lib.router.js` | **150+ endpoints** mounted at `/` — RFQ, vendor, product CRUD. Never read whole — grep or use offset/limit |
51	| `routes/admin.router.js` | `/api/admin` — app settings, section 301, HTS rates, Trump 2025, factories, impersonation |
52	| `routes/auth.router.js` | `/auth` — Azure SSO login |
53	| `routes/notification.router.js` | `/api/notifications` |
54	| `routes/user.router.js` | `/api/user` — profile, photo upload |
55	| `routes/openrouter.router.js` | `/api/openrouter` — AI proxy |
56	| `routes/cross.router.js` | `/api/cross` — CBP CROSS rulings proxy |
57	| `routes/comment.router.js` | `/api/comment` |
58	| `routes/customer.router.js` | `/api/customers` |
59	| `routes/grid.router.js` | `/api/grid` |
60	| `routes/health.router.js` | `/api/health` |
61	| `routes/digitalocean.router.js` | File storage endpoints mounted at `/` |
62	| `routes/` (others) | `itemSize`, `itemDepth`, `std`, `role`, `autofill` |
63	| `services/` | Business logic layer — Sequelize queries and domain operations |
64	| `controllers/` | Thin route handlers; delegate to services or `models/lib.model.js` |
65	| `tests/unit/` | Jest unit tests (227 tests across 19 suites, hermetic, runs in ~1 s) |
66	| `data/hts-rates.json` | Bundled USITC HTS schedule (~1 MB, ~13.6 k codes) — regenerated yearly by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` |
67	| `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` | One-shot downloader to rebuild `data/hts-rates.json` from USITC |
68	| `docs/` | `architecture.md`, `configuration.md`, `development.md`, `deployment.md` |
69	| `.env.example` | Template for local `.env.<env-name>` files |
70	| `Dockerfile` | Single-stage Node production image |
71	| `cloudbuild.yaml` | Google Cloud Build CI/CD definition |
72	| `node_modules/` | Third-party packages — **never edit** |
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 4. The Prime Directive
77	
78	The entire repo is custom code — there is no committed vendor framework directory analogous to the frontend's `src/@vex/`. Everything under the repo root is fair game **except**:
79	
80	- `node_modules/` — never edit.
81	- `models/db/*.js` model files are effectively schema declarations. When you add a column to a model, you **must** also add the matching `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js` or the column will never exist in production. See §6 Decision Tree.
82	- `routes/lib.router.js` and `models/lib.model.js` are giant legacy files (150+ endpoints, ~3000 lines). **Add to them surgically rather than refactoring.** Any refactor that touches most of the file needs a coordinated effort — not a solo AI session.
83	
84	---
85	
86	## 5. Core modification inventory
87	
88	No third-party or framework files outside our directories have been intentionally modified. There is no vendor layer to track here.
89	
90	| File | Change made | Why | Risk |
91	|---|---|---|---|
92	| — | — | — | — |
93	
94	---
95	
96	## 6. Decision tree
97	
98	**Add a new API endpoint:**
99	1. Choose the right router (`lib.router.js` for RFQ/product/vendor; a named router for anything else)
100	2. Add a thin handler in `controllers/`; keep business logic in `services/` or `models/lib.model.js`
101	3. Gate with `authRole([...roles])` from `helpers/auth.js`
102	4. Add a Jest test in `tests/unit/`
103	5. Document in `docs/architecture.md` § Routes
104	
105	**Add a column to a database table:**
106	1. Add the field to the Sequelize model in `models/db/<Model>.js`
107	2. Add `sequelize.query(\`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE\`).catch(...)` inside `initialize()` in `models/db.js`. **If the column name is mixed-case (e.g. `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`), it MUST be double-quoted in the ALTER TABLE.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case name is folded to lowercase by Postgres and Sequelize never finds it — `findAll` then throws and the grid breaks (see §14, SYS-002).
108	3. Add a backfill query in the same block if needed (idempotent — guard with `WHERE col IS NULL`)
109	4. Add a regression assertion in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`
110	5. **Do not skip step 2.** `sequelize.sync()` does NOT alter existing tables. Missing this step causes "column does not exist" crashes in production (see §14, SYS-030).
111	
112	**Add a new Sequelize model (new table):**
113	1. Create `models/db/<Model>.js` following the existing pattern
114	2. Register in `models/db/init-models.js`
115	3. Write a `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js`
116	
117	**Change auth or role logic:**
118	1. Auth middleware: `helpers/auth.js` (`authRole`, `getUserInfo`)
119	2. Role names come from the JWT payload: `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`
120	3. Context (per-request user without prop-drilling): `helpers/context.middleware.js`
121	4. Do not add route-level auth bypass — all protected routes must use `authRole`
122	
123	**Change admin impersonation:**
124	1. Endpoint: `controllers/admin.controller.js` `startImpersonation`, mounted by `routes/admin.router.js` at `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`
125	2. Normal user rows resolve `:userId` against `users.id`
126	3. Visible factory rows send `{ targetType: 'factory' }` and resolve `:userId` against `Factory.id`; this is the primary Factories & Reps path because the frontend may not expose a master-detail expand button.
127	4. Nested rep rows, if shown, send `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` and resolve `:userId` against `vendor.vendor_id`
128	5. Vendor/factory impersonation tokens must include `role: 'vendor'`, `access`, `factory_id`, and `impersonated_by`. For visible factory rows, `factory_id` is `Factory.id` and `email` may be `null`; downstream vendor endpoints must not require `vendor_email`.
129	6. `Customer.getRFQStep` must fall back to the JWT `role` when no user/vendor row resolves, otherwise factory-row impersonation can crash Cloud Run with `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'level')`.
130	7. Add/update `tests/unit/admin.impersonation.test.js` and `tests/unit/rfqStep.vendor-token.test.js` when changing this flow
131	
132	**Add a persistent app setting (survives redeploy):**
133	1. Use `services/app-setting.service.js` (`get(key)` / `set(key, value)`)
134	2. The `app_settings` table is the canonical key/value store (see §8)
135	3. For data that is static per calendar year and the same for every customer, prefer a bundled JSON file over an admin-managed upload (`data/hts-rates.json` is the model)
136	
137	**Add or change a tariff lookup (Section 301 / MFN / Trump 2025):**
138	- Section 301: `helpers/section301.js` + `routes/admin.router.js`
139	- MFN base rate: `helpers/hts-rates.js` (read from `data/hts-rates.json`); re-run `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` yearly
140	- Trump 2025: `helpers/trump2025.js` + `app_settings['trump_2025_tariffs']`
141	
142	**Refresh the bundled HTS schedule:**
143	```bash
144	node scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js
145	```
146	Run once per year when USITC publishes a new revision. Commit the updated `data/hts-rates.json`.
147	
148	**Add an email notification:**
149	1. `helpers/brevo.js` — Brevo transactional email wrapper
150	2. `services/notification.service.js` — in-app notification store
151	3. `helpers/mention.js` — @mention pipeline (auto-invoked from `addComment`)
152	
153	---
154	
155	## 7. Task-to-file navigation map
156	
157	| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
158	|---|---|---|
159	| Add an RFQ/vendor/product endpoint | `routes/lib.router.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js` or `services/` | Other routers unless the feature clearly belongs elsewhere |
160	| Add an admin endpoint | `routes/admin.router.js`, `controllers/admin.controller.js` | `lib.router.js` |
161	| Change admin impersonation | `controllers/admin.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js` if vendor-token identity is affected, `routes/admin.router.js`, `tests/unit/admin.impersonation.test.js`, `tests/unit/rfqStep.vendor-token.test.js`, `docs/architecture.md` | `helpers/auth.js` unless role verification itself changes |
162	| Add/change a DB column | `models/db/<Model>.js`, `models/db.js` (migration), `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` | Any other file unless the column needs a new service method |
163	| Add a tariff helper | `helpers/<name>.js`, corresponding test in `tests/unit/` | Route files (add endpoint separately) |
164	| Change auth pipeline | `helpers/auth.js`, `index.js` (if the middleware swap changes) | Route files (auth is middleware, not per-route logic) |
165	| Change file storage behaviour | `helpers/digitalocean.js`, `routes/digitalocean.router.js` | `helpers/multer.js` (unless upload constraints change) |
166	| Add an AI model/provider | `helpers/openrouter.js` | Route files (proxy is transparent to the route) |
167	| Change the notification service | `services/notification.service.js`, `routes/notification.router.js` | `helpers/mention.js` (only if @mention pipeline changes) |
168	| Update Trump 2025 rates shape | `helpers/trump2025.js` (validation), `routes/admin.router.js`, `services/app-setting.service.js` | Frontend (consumes lookup endpoint only) |
169	| Change build or deploy | `cloudbuild.yaml`, `Dockerfile` | `config/env.js` (only if adding a new environment) |
170	| Add environment variable | `.env.example`, `docs/configuration.md`, the relevant `cloudbuild.yaml` `--set-env-vars` or `--set-secrets` block | Hardcode into source |
171	| Add a Jest test | `tests/unit/<feature>.test.js` | Any source file (tests are read-only against the source) |
172	
173	---
174	
175	## 8. Data model
176	
177	See `docs/architecture.md` for full Sequelize model details. Key entities:
178	
179	| Entity | Key fields | Where defined | Notes |
180	|---|---|---|---|
181	| `users` | `user_id`, `email`, `name`, `role`, `profile_photo` | `models/db/users.js` | `profile_photo` was added via safe migration (SYS-030 — see §14). Auto-provisioned on first Azure SSO login with `level: 'designer'`. |
182	| `Customer` | `customer_id`, `customer_name` | `models/db/Customer.js` | Confusingly used as the top-level namespace in `models/lib.model.js` |
183	| `Vendor` | `vendor_id`, `vendor_country`, `factory_id_fk`, `vendor_access` | `models/db/Vendor.js` | FK to `Factory`; `vendor_country` is the per-vendor country override. Rep impersonation resolves by `vendor_id` when the request body has `{ targetType: 'vendor' }`; visible factory-row impersonation resolves `Factory.id` when `targetType` is `factory` and can produce a vendor-scoped token with `email: null`. |
184	| `Factory` | `factory_id`, `factory_nickname`, `factory_country` | `models/db/Factory.js` | `factory_country` added May 2026 via safe migration; backfilled from most common `vendor_country` |
185	| `RFQVendor` | `RFQVendor_id`, `RFQitem_id_fk`, `vendor_id_fk`, `RFQVendor_amount`, `quote_date`, `req_status`, `requote_requested` | `models/db/RFQVendor.js` | One factory's quote on one RFQ item. `RFQVendor_id` is the `quote_id` the frontend sends. **`vendor_id_fk` stores `Factory.id`, not `Vendor.vendor_id`** (see §11). `requote_requested` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06 via safe migration) flags a stale price for refresh; mapped in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem`. |
186	| `RFQItem` | `rfqItem_id`, `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation`, `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, `Price Code` | `models/db/RFQItem.js` | `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` stores the raw 3-term duty breakdown (e.g. `"5.3+25+10"`). `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` (TEXT/JSON) maps each `*_price_sales` colId → `{duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}`, snapshotting the cost inputs when a "Price for Sales" cell was populated; mapped both directions in `helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem`/`mapRFQItem`), mirroring `rfqItem_requested_price_cells`. |
187	| `app_settings` | `key`, `value` (VARCHAR, stores JSON) | `models/db/AppSetting.js` | Managed by `services/app-setting.service.js`; see §6 for key inventory |
188	| `user_notification` | `id`, `type`, `event`, `title`, `message`, `unread`, `user_id_fk` | `models/db/UserNotification.js` | Managed by `services/notification.service.js` |
189	
190	**`app_settings` key inventory:**
191	
192	| Key | Value shape | Written by |
193	|---|---|---|
194	| `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin → AI Settings |
195	| `section301_lookup` | JSON `{ "HTS": { list, rate, description } }` (~1 MB) | Admin → Section 301 CSV upload; legacy PDF endpoint still wired but UI-hidden |
196	| `section301_overrides` | JSON `{ "NNNN.NN.NN": { list, rate, description? } }` | Admin → Section 301 Overrides; consulted before parsed lookup data |
197	| `trump_2025_tariffs` | JSON `[{ country, rate }, ...]` | Admin → Trump 2025 Tariffs editor |
198	
199	---
200	
201	## 9. Container inventory
202	
203	| Container | Purpose | Managed by | Image tag pattern |
204	|---|---|---|---|
205	| `designflow-backend` (this repo) | Core REST API | Google Cloud Run | `{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT}/{REPO}/{ENV}/backend:{BUILD_ID}-{SHA}` |
206	
207	**Sibling services (separate repos — not documented here):**
208	
209	| Service | Role |
210	|---|---|
211	| `designflow-bff` | Terminates user JWT; adds OIDC token; forwards to this service |
212	| `designflow-item-master` | Item master data service |
213	| `designflow-data-sync` | Data syncing service |
214	
215	Cloud Build substitution variables (`cloudbuild.yaml`): `_REGION`, `_SERVICE_NAME`, `_APP_ENV`, `_APP_NAME`, `_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME`, `_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_NAME`, `_PORT`, `_CPU`, `_MEMORY`, `_CONCURRENCY`, `_MAX_INSTANCES`. All passed at trigger time — none hardcoded.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## 10. What to ignore
220	
221	Do not index or deeply analyze these paths:
222	
223	```
224	node_modules/
225	coverage/
226	data/hts-rates.json   ← data, not code; ~1 MB; AI context budget wasted here
227	```
228	
229	The `data/hts-rates.json` file is a generated snapshot. When you need to understand its shape, read the first 10 lines or the `helpers/hts-rates.js` comments — not the full file.
230	
231	---
232	
233	## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions
234	
235	### `lib.router.js` and `lib.model.js` are giant files
236	
237	**Looks like:** Poor modularization — 150+ endpoints in one file; `lib.model.js` is ~3000 lines.
238	
239	**Actually:** Organic legacy growth. There is no formal split planned.
240	
241	**Do not change because:** Splitting would touch every import and require a coordinated, carefully tested migration. The API surface is still in flux.
242	
243	---
244	
245	### `models/lib.model.js` — namespace is called `Customer` but contains everything
246	
247	**Looks like:** Misnamed — the file handles vendors, factories, RFQ items, std products, art pieces, etc.
248	
249	**Actually:** `Customer` was the first domain object. Everything else accreted around it.
250	
251	**Do not change because:** The namespace name is internal. Renaming would touch every call-site for no user-facing benefit.
252	
253	---
254	
255	### Migrations live in `models/db.js`, not a migrations folder
256	
257	**Looks like:** The wrong place for schema changes.
258	
259	**Actually:** There is no `sequelize-cli`. The team uses inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` queries because they are idempotent and self-applying on every deploy.
260	
261	**Pattern:**
262	```js
263	// Inside initialize() in models/db.js, after sequelize.sync():
264	sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE`)
265	  .catch(err => console.warn('Migration <name> skipped:', err.message));
266	```
267	
268	`${schema}` resolves to `"designflow".` in prod (or empty in test envs). Always add a matching assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
269	
270	**Quote any mixed-case column identifier** (`"rfqItem_col"`), not just the table name. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case column is folded to lowercase by Postgres, producing an orphan column Sequelize can never query (this caused SYS-002 — see §14). Existing migrations already do this (e.g. `"rfqItem_duty_rate_equation"`, `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). All-lowercase names (e.g. `factory_country`, `profile_photo`) don't need quoting, but quoting them anyway is harmless and consistent.
271	
272	---
273	
274	### `pdf-parse` is lazy-required in `helpers/section301.js`
275	
276	**Looks like:** Sloppy — package imported inside a function, not at module top.
277	
278	**Actually:** `pdf-parse@1.1.1` has known load-time issues on certain Node versions; lazy-require defers the cost to the first PDF upload. v2.x is broken in Node — stay pinned to `1.1.1`.
279	
280	**Do not change because:** Eager-requiring it can crash startup.
281	
282	---
283	
284	### `index.js` swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization`
285	
286	**Looks like:** Odd first middleware that overwrites a standard header.
287	
288	**Actually:** The BFF puts the OIDC service-to-service token in `Authorization` (so Cloud Run can read it) and the user JWT in `X-User-Authorization`. Cloud Run strips the OIDC token. This swap restores the user JWT to where `helpers/auth.js` expects it. **Without this swap, no route handler sees the user.**
289	
290	**Do not change because:** Removing it breaks all authenticated routes.
291	
292	---
293	
294	### `cross-env NODE_ENV=...` in every start script
295	
296	**Looks like:** Redundant `package.json` scripts for each environment.
297	
298	**Actually:** Each script loads a different `.env.<env-name>` via `config/env.js`. Adding a new environment = new script + new `.env.<env-name>` file.
299	
300	---
301	
302	### `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` not in `--set-env-vars` in `cloudbuild.yaml`
303	
304	**Looks like:** Missing configuration.
305	
306	**Actually:** `--set-env-vars` in Cloud Build is a **full replacement** — anything not listed is wiped on next deploy. API keys and secrets belong in `--set-secrets` (Secret Manager refs), which persist independently. See §13 and §14.
307	
308	---
309	
310	### DeepSeek direct API alongside OpenRouter for AI classification
311	
312	**Looks like:** Two separate AI client files (`helpers/openrouter.js` and `helpers/deepseek.js`) that do similar things — redundant.
313	
314	**Actually:** DeepSeek is available via OpenRouter, but routing directly to `api.deepseek.com` is cheaper (no OpenRouter markup) and exposes the `thinking` parameter directly. The `reasoning_effort` field (`'none'|'high'|'max'`) maps to DeepSeek's `thinking: { type, budget_tokens }` internally; OpenRouter may not expose this parameter.
315	
316	**How routing works:** `/api/openrouter/chat` checks the `api` field in the request body:
317	- `api = 'deepseek-direct'` → `helpers/deepseek.js`
318	- `api` omitted + model starts with `deepseek/` + `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set → auto-routes direct
319	- Everything else → `helpers/openrouter.js`
320	
321	**`DEEPSEEK_DIRECT_MODELS`** in `routes/openrouter.router.js` is a hardcoded model list that mirrors OpenRouter's `/models` response shape. The frontend reads it at `GET /api/openrouter/models?api=deepseek-direct`. Includes `prompt_cache` pricing (~10% of input price) for accurate per-turn cost tracking.
322	
323	**Do not change because:** DeepSeek's thinking-mode pricing (~$0.14/1M input, ~$0.014/1M cache hit) is meaningfully cheaper than going through OpenRouter for high-volume classifications.
324	
325	---
326	
327	### `RFQVendor.vendor_id_fk` holds a `Factory.id`, not a `Vendor.vendor_id`
328	
329	**Looks like:** A foreign key to the `Vendor` table, given the column name.
330	
331	**Actually:** It stores `Factory.id`. A `Factory` has many `Vendor` contact rows; the quote belongs to the factory, not an individual contact. `Customer.reqVendor` resolves the factory id from the vendor's `factory_id_fk` before writing it (`resolvedFactoryId = vendor_info.factory_id_fk`). The vendor JWT also carries `factory_id` (signed in `makeSingleToken` and the login path), so the two line up for the ownership guard below.
332	
333	**Do not change because:** Renaming the column or "fixing" it to a real vendor id would break every quote lookup, the chosen-vendor logic, and `emailVendorsForFactory`.
334	
335	---
336	
337	### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open
338	
339	**Looks like:** `helpers/quote-access.js` `vendorMayModifyQuote()` returns `true` in several cases instead of strictly denying — looks like a leaky check.
340	
341	**Actually:** `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) is a sequential integer, so a malicious vendor could enumerate other factories' quotes and POST `updateVendor` / `keepPrice` against them (IDOR). The guard blocks the one real case — a `vendor` whose token `factory_id` differs from the quote's `vendor_id_fk` — and deliberately **fails open** for internal roles, vendors with no `factory_id` on their token (legacy records), and quote rows with no `vendor_id_fk`. The intent is to close the enumeration hole without ever locking out a legitimate user (notably the invite-an-individual-vendor-to-one-item flow, where the invited factory owns its own quote row).
342	
343	**Do not change because:** Making it fail closed would block legacy vendors whose records were never linked to a factory, and `requestRequote` already hard-gates to internal roles separately. If you tighten it, backfill `factory_id` on all vendor records first.
344	
345	---
346	
347	### Trump 2025 `normalizeCountry` alias table is hand-curated
348	
349	**Looks like:** Fragile string matching.
350	
351	**Actually:** The alias table (`"PRC"` → `"china"`, `"Peoples Republic of China"` → `"china"`, etc.) is intentionally small and hand-curated. Admins should add factory countries under the name they use in their FOB Country master list to avoid alias gaps.
352	
353	---
354	
355	## 12. Environment and credentials
356	
357	See `docs/configuration.md` for the full variable list. Key variables:
358	
359	| Variable | Purpose | Where it lives |
360	|---|---|---|
361	| `DATABASE_URL` / `DB_*` | PostgreSQL connection | `.env.<env>` / Cloud Run secrets |
362	| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` / `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` | RS256 signing | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
363	| `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` / `AZURE_TENANT_ID` | Azure JWKS verification | `.env.<env>` / `--set-env-vars` |
364	| `SCHEMA` | PostgreSQL schema name (default: `designflow`) | `.env.<env>` |
365	| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | AI proxy (OpenRouter) | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
366	| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | DeepSeek direct API | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` (`deepseek-api-key:latest`) |
367	| `DO_*` / `DIGITAL_OCEAN_*` | DigitalOcean Spaces | `.env.<env>` / secrets |
368	| `BREVO_API_KEY` | Transactional email | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
369	| `PORT` | Express listen port (default 5000) | `.env.<env>` / `--set-env-vars` |
370	| `NODE_ENV` | Selects `.env.<NODE_ENV>` file | `package.json` start script |
371	
372	**Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`.** That flag is a full replacement on every deploy — any value not listed is deleted. Secrets go in `--set-secrets` (Cloud Run Secret Manager references), which persist independently.
373	
374	---
375	
376	## 13. Deployment
377	
378	See `docs/deployment.md` for the full pipeline. Summary:
379	
380	- **CI/CD:** Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`) — no GitHub Actions
381	- **Build:** Single-stage Node `Dockerfile`; image pushed to Google Artifact Registry
382	- **Runtime:** Google Cloud Run (managed, serverless, stateless)
383	- **Deploy trigger:** Cloud Build trigger on push to the relevant branch
384	- **Rollback:** Cloud Run revision traffic split via `gcloud run services update-traffic`
385	- **Env vars:** Plain vars in `--set-env-vars` (full replacement per deploy); secrets in `--set-secrets` (persistent)
386	- **Branch policy:** `sandbox-albert` → sandbox Cloud Run service. Never merge directly to `develop` — PRs only.
387	
388	---
389	
390	## 14. Critical incident log
391	
392	### 2026-05-29 — SYS-002: RFQ grid empty after unquoted `price_sales_snapshots` migration
393	
394	**What happened:** The RFQ grid showed "no rows to show" with a "Something went wrong (SYS-002)" toast. Every RFQ load failed.
395	
396	**Root cause:** The `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` migration in `models/db.js` was written with an **unquoted** identifier (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`). Postgres folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase, so it created `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots`. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so `RFQItem.findAll` queried the case-sensitive `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"` that never existed → `findAll` threw → `Customer.getAll` propagated the error to `controllers/main.controller.js` `findAll`, which returned `sendError(res, err, 'SYS-002')` → empty grid. Every other migration in the file already quoted its identifier; this was the lone exception. The shipped unit test had asserted the *unquoted* form, so CI green-lit the bug.
397	
398	**Recovery:** Quoted the identifier (commit `a556d3f` → `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`) and added `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem" DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots"` (commit `476e453`) to remove the empty lowercase orphan column. Corrected `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` to require the quoted form and added guards: fail on the unquoted form, assert the orphan DROP exists, assert the correct mixed-case column is never dropped.
399	
400	**Rule:** Inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migrations in `models/db.js` **must quote any mixed-case identifier**. Sequelize quotes identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase, creating an orphan column Sequelize can never find. See §6 and §11.
401	
402	---
403	
404	### 2026 — SYS-030: `profile_photo` column missing in production, login crashes
405	
406	**What happened:** `profile_photo` was added to the `users` Sequelize model (`models/db/users.js`) in commit `53cc531` without adding the matching `ALTER TABLE` migration in `models/db.js`. Every `sql.users.findOne()` in production failed with `column "profile_photo" does not exist`, crashing login.
407	
408	**Root cause:** Sequelize `.sync()` does not alter existing tables. Adding a field to a model without a corresponding migration is a silent no-op in dev (if the dev DB already has the column) but fails in prod.
409	
410	**Recovery:** Added the `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration to `models/db.js` and deployed.
411	
412	**Rule:** Whenever you add a field to a Sequelize model in `models/db/*.js`, you **must** also add `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `models/db.js`. Add an assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
413	
414	---
415	
416	### 2026 — SSO 401: BFF OIDC audience mismatch (recurring — at least 3 times)
417	
418	**What happened:** `POST /auth/azure` returned 401. Cloud Run logged: `The request was not authorized to invoke this service. The access token could not be verified.`
419	
420	**Root cause:** The BFF uses `CORE_BACKEND_URL` as both the proxy target and the OIDC token `aud` claim. Cloud Run validates `aud` against the service's `.run.app` URL. Custom domains route traffic correctly via Cloud Run domain mapping **but do not work as OIDC audiences**. When `CORE_BACKEND_URL` was set to a custom domain, every OIDC token was rejected.
421	
422	**Recovery:** Update `CORE_BACKEND_URL` (and `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, etc.) on the BFF Cloud Run service config and `designflow-bff/env/.env.*` to the `.run.app` service URL.
423	
424	**Triage commands:**
425	```bash
426	# Check current BFF env vars on Cloud Run
427	gcloud run services describe designflow-bff --region=<REGION> --format='value(spec.template.spec.containers[0].env)'
428	
429	# Update CORE_BACKEND_URL
430	gcloud run services update designflow-bff --region=<REGION> \
431	  --update-env-vars CORE_BACKEND_URL=https://<service-name>-<hash>-<region>.a.run.app
432	```
433	
434	**Rule:** BFF `*_BACKEND_URL` values must **always** be `.run.app` service URLs, never custom domains. This applies to `CORE_BACKEND_URL`, `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, and any future sibling service URL. This has been the root cause of SSO outages at least 3 times.
435	
436	---
437	
438	### 2026 — Secrets wiped after deploy: `--set-env-vars` is a full replacement
439	
440	**What happened:** A secret added manually to the Cloud Run service via the console disappeared after the next `cloudbuild.yaml` deploy.
441	
442	**Root cause:** `--set-env-vars` in `gcloud run services update` replaces the entire set of plain env vars on the service. Any variable not in the flag is deleted.
443	
444	**Recovery:** Move secrets to Secret Manager. Reference them in `cloudbuild.yaml` via `--set-secrets` (e.g. `--set-secrets=OPENROUTER_API_KEY=openrouter-api-key:latest`), which persists independently across deploys.
445	
446	**Rule:** Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`. All credentials go through Secret Manager + `--set-secrets`. Only non-secret config goes in `--set-env-vars`, and every variable that must survive a deploy must be listed there on every deploy.
447	
448	---
449	
450	### 2026-06-05 — Garbled Chinese in RFQ sub-grid headers: CP437 mojibake in `designflow_dev`
451	
452	**What happened:** The RFQ detail/sub-grid column headers rendered as garbage (`Σ╗╖µá╝µèÑΣ╗╖ Quote Price`, `τ║╕τ«▒Θò┐σ║ª cm Carton length`) while hardcoded Chinese button labels in the same component (`detail-cell-renderer.component.html`, `rfq-button.component.html`) rendered correctly.
453	
454	**Root cause:** The header text was **corrupt at rest in the database**, not a frontend or DB-connection bug. The Chinese was double-encoded: original UTF-8 bytes were decoded through **CP437** (the US Windows / `cmd.exe` OEM console codepage) and then re-saved as UTF-8. The `GridLayout` `rfq_detail` headers were just the *visible* symptom — a full scan (`scripts/scan-mojibake-everywhere.js`) showed the **entire `designflow_dev` schema is pervasively corrupted: ~35,000+ rows across 23+ text columns** (AuditLog, email_logs, RFQItem, RFQVendor, Factory, vendor, externalVendor, itemHeader, itemAttachment, licensingStatus, merchGroup, users, …). It affects all non-ASCII text — Chinese, accented Latin (`Décor`→`D├⌐cor`, `Cortázar`→`Cort├ízar`), symbols (`©`→`┬⌐`), smart quotes (`'`→`ΓÇÖ`), fullwidth punctuation, and NBSP. The corruption is **confined to `designflow_dev`**; the `designflow` and `designflow_sandbox` schemas scanned 100% clean (they hold the correct originals). This confirms the whole `designflow_dev` schema was hand-seeded through the bad CP437 console in one go. `SHOW server_encoding`/`client_encoding` were both already `UTF8`, so the connection was never the cause. The `designflow-data-syncing` service is NOT the culprit (it runs on Linux, which cannot produce CP437, and its `GridLayout` model is hardcoded to `schema: 'designflow_sandbox'`). The corruption came from a **one-time manual seed of `designflow_dev` run through a Windows console** (psql/pg_dump piped through `cmd`/PowerShell at codepage 437, or Chinese pasted into an interactive `cmd` psql session).
455	
456	**How to recognize it:** Text shows box-drawing/Greek/accented glyphs (`Σ τ µ σ Φ ╗ ╝ ║ ▒ ░`). Reverse the double-encode with `iconv.encode(value, 'cp437').toString('utf8')` — if it yields clean CJK, this is the bug. (Use `cp437`, not `cp850` — cp850 fails on these rows.)
457	
458	**Recovery:** Repaired the 12 visible `designflow_dev.GridLayout` header rows via the cp437 reverse-transform (scripts: `scripts/fix-mojibake-headers.*`, `scripts/scan-mojibake-everywhere.js`; backup JSON kept in `scripts/`). Added `client_encoding: 'UTF8'` to `dialectOptions` in `models/db.js` here and in `designflow-data-syncing/models/db.js` as defense-in-depth. **The remaining ~35k corrupted rows across the rest of `designflow_dev` were NOT auto-fixed** — a schema-wide reverse-transform needs per-value detection (skip already-clean rows, validate each result) + full backup; the safest fix is usually to re-seed `designflow_dev` server-side from the clean `designflow_sandbox` schema rather than transform in place.
459	
460	**Rule:** **Never seed/copy `designflow_dev` (or any schema) by piping `pg_dump`/`psql` through a Windows console.** Multibyte text gets silently mangled by the OEM codepage. To (re)seed from the clean source in the same DB, copy server-side where text never touches a client codepage:
461	```sql
462	INSERT INTO designflow_dev."GridLayout"
463	SELECT * FROM designflow_sandbox."GridLayout"
464	ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
465	```
466	If a dump/restore on Windows is unavoidable, force UTF-8 end to end: `chcp 65001`, `set PGCLIENTENCODING=UTF8`, and use `psql -f file.sql` (file I/O is UTF-8) rather than `<` redirection or pasting into the console.
467	
468	---
469	
470	## 15. Pending work
471	
472	| Status | Item | Next action |
473	|---|---|---|
474	| pending | Notification trigger points — nothing calls `createNotification()` on business events | Add `NotificationService.createNotification()` calls in the backend at task assignment, status change, and approval events. Service API is in `services/notification.service.js`; endpoints are ready in `routes/notification.router.js`. |
475

file.content:
# AGENTS.md — Designflow Backend: Primary Developer Guide

Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository.

---

## 1. What this service is

Designflow Backend is the main REST API for the Designflow PLM platform. It is a stateless Node.js + Express service backed by PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM). Identity comes from a JWT on every request; session state does not exist. The service runs on Google Cloud Run and is reached exclusively through the BFF (`designflow-bff`), which terminates the user JWT and adds an OIDC service-to-service token before forwarding. The frontend is a separate Angular SPA in a sibling repository. For a full system diagram see `docs/architecture.md`.

---

## 2. Multi-model note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the "What to ignore" section below.

---

## 3. Repository structure

| Path | What it is |
|---|---|
| `index.js` | Server entry — middleware stack (including the `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` swap) and `listen` |
| `config/env.js` | dotenv loader; resolves `.env.${NODE_ENV}` |
| `config/db.config.js` | PostgreSQL connection params; throws on missing required key |
| `config/digitalocean.config.js` | DigitalOcean Spaces credentials |
| `config/brevo.config.js` | Brevo email credentials |
| `helpers/auth.js` | JWT RS256 verify; `authRole(roles[])` middleware factory; `getUserInfo(token)` |
| `helpers/context.middleware.js` | `AsyncLocalStorage` per-request store `{ user, reqId }` — lets deep-stack code read the current user without prop-drilling |
| `helpers/openrouter.js` | OpenRouter API client; prompt-caching markers for anthropic/deepseek/google models |
| `helpers/deepseek.js` | DeepSeek direct API client (`api.deepseek.com`); maps `reasoning_effort: 'none'\|'high'\|'max'` to the DeepSeek `thinking` parameter; used when `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set and `api='deepseek-direct'` |
| `helpers/section301.js` | Section 301 PDF text extraction + regex HTS parser (lazy-requires `pdf-parse`) |
| `helpers/hts-rates.js` | MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC HTS JSON (`data/hts-rates.json`) |
| `helpers/trump2025.js` | Trump 2025 reciprocal-tariff lookup; alias-aware country normalization |
| `helpers/factories.js` | Factory list / country map / `updateCountry(id, country)` |
| `helpers/digitalocean.js` | S3 client; presigned URL, ACL, upload, delete |
| `helpers/mention.js` | `parseMentions` / `resolveMentions` for @mention pipeline |
| `helpers/multer.js` | Multer instances (memory storage) configured per upload type |
| `helpers/errorHandler.js` | Express error-handler middleware |
| `helpers/quote-access.js` | `vendorMayModifyQuote(userInfo, rfqVendor)` — ownership guard preventing a vendor from mutating another factory's quote by `quote_id` (see §11) |
| `helpers/utility.js` | Shared utility functions |
| `models/db.js` | Sequelize init + model registration + **all schema migrations** (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`) |
| `models/db/` | 60+ Sequelize model files (one per table) |
| `models/lib.model.js` | Legacy domain-method module (`Customer` namespace — see §11) |
| `routes/index.js` | Root router — mounts all subrouters |
| `routes/lib.router.js` | **150+ endpoints** mounted at `/` — RFQ, vendor, product CRUD. Never read whole — grep or use offset/limit |
| `routes/admin.router.js` | `/api/admin` — app settings, section 301, HTS rates, Trump 2025, factories, impersonation |
| `routes/auth.router.js` | `/auth` — Azure SSO login |
| `routes/notification.router.js` | `/api/notifications` |
| `routes/user.router.js` | `/api/user` — profile, photo upload |
| `routes/openrouter.router.js` | `/api/openrouter` — AI proxy |
| `routes/cross.router.js` | `/api/cross` — CBP CROSS rulings proxy |
| `routes/comment.router.js` | `/api/comment` |
| `routes/customer.router.js` | `/api/customers` |
| `routes/grid.router.js` | `/api/grid` |
| `routes/health.router.js` | `/api/health` |
| `routes/digitalocean.router.js` | File storage endpoints mounted at `/` |
| `routes/` (others) | `itemSize`, `itemDepth`, `std`, `role`, `autofill` |
| `services/` | Business logic layer — Sequelize queries and domain operations |
| `controllers/` | Thin route handlers; delegate to services or `models/lib.model.js` |
| `tests/unit/` | Jest unit tests (227 tests across 19 suites, hermetic, runs in ~1 s) |
| `data/hts-rates.json` | Bundled USITC HTS schedule (~1 MB, ~13.6 k codes) — regenerated yearly by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` |
| `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` | One-shot downloader to rebuild `data/hts-rates.json` from USITC |
| `docs/` | `architecture.md`, `configuration.md`, `development.md`, `deployment.md` |
| `.env.example` | Template for local `.env.<env-name>` files |
| `Dockerfile` | Single-stage Node production image |
| `cloudbuild.yaml` | Google Cloud Build CI/CD definition |
| `node_modules/` | Third-party packages — **never edit** |

---

## 4. The Prime Directive

The entire repo is custom code — there is no committed vendor framework directory analogous to the frontend's `src/@vex/`. Everything under the repo root is fair game **except**:

- `node_modules/` — never edit.
- `models/db/*.js` model files are effectively schema declarations. When you add a column to a model, you **must** also add the matching `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js` or the column will never exist in production. See §6 Decision Tree.
- `routes/lib.router.js` and `models/lib.model.js` are giant legacy files (150+ endpoints, ~3000 lines). **Add to them surgically rather than refactoring.** Any refactor that touches most of the file needs a coordinated effort — not a solo AI session.

---

## 5. Core modification inventory

No third-party or framework files outside our directories have been intentionally modified. There is no vendor layer to track here.

| File | Change made | Why | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| — | — | — | — |

---

## 6. Decision tree

**Add a new API endpoint:**
1. Choose the right router (`lib.router.js` for RFQ/product/vendor; a named router for anything else)
2. Add a thin handler in `controllers/`; keep business logic in `services/` or `models/lib.model.js`
3. Gate with `authRole([...roles])` from `helpers/auth.js`
4. Add a Jest test in `tests/unit/`
5. Document in `docs/architecture.md` § Routes

**Add a column to a database table:**
1. Add the field to the Sequelize model in `models/db/<Model>.js`
2. Add `sequelize.query(\`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE\`).catch(...)` inside `initialize()` in `models/db.js`. **If the column name is mixed-case (e.g. `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`), it MUST be double-quoted in the ALTER TABLE.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case name is folded to lowercase by Postgres and Sequelize never finds it — `findAll` then throws and the grid breaks (see §14, SYS-002).
3. Add a backfill query in the same block if needed (idempotent — guard with `WHERE col IS NULL`)
4. Add a regression assertion in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`
5. **Do not skip step 2.** `sequelize.sync()` does NOT alter existing tables. Missing this step causes "column does not exist" crashes in production (see §14, SYS-030).

**Add a new Sequelize model (new table):**
1. Create `models/db/<Model>.js` following the existing pattern
2. Register in `models/db/init-models.js`
3. Write a `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js`

**Change auth or role logic:**
1. Auth middleware: `helpers/auth.js` (`authRole`, `getUserInfo`)
2. Role names come from the JWT payload: `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`
3. Context (per-request user without prop-drilling): `helpers/context.middleware.js`
4. Do not add route-level auth bypass — all protected routes must use `authRole`

**Change admin impersonation:**
1. Endpoint: `controllers/admin.controller.js` `startImpersonation`, mounted by `routes/admin.router.js` at `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`
2. Normal user rows resolve `:userId` against `users.id`
3. Visible factory rows send `{ targetType: 'factory' }` and resolve `:userId` against `Factory.id`; this is the primary Factories & Reps path because the frontend may not expose a master-detail expand button.
4. Nested rep rows, if shown, send `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` and resolve `:userId` against `vendor.vendor_id`
5. Vendor/factory impersonation tokens must include `role: 'vendor'`, `access`, `factory_id`, and `impersonated_by`. For visible factory rows, `factory_id` is `Factory.id` and `email` may be `null`; downstream vendor endpoints must not require `vendor_email`.
6. `Customer.getRFQStep` must fall back to the JWT `role` when no user/vendor row resolves, otherwise factory-row impersonation can crash Cloud Run with `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'level')`.
7. Add/update `tests/unit/admin.impersonation.test.js` and `tests/unit/rfqStep.vendor-token.test.js` when changing this flow

**Add a persistent app setting (survives redeploy):**
1. Use `services/app-setting.service.js` (`get(key)` / `set(key, value)`)
2. The `app_settings` table is the canonical key/value store (see §8)
3. For data that is static per calendar year and the same for every customer, prefer a bundled JSON file over an admin-managed upload (`data/hts-rates.json` is the model)

**Add or change a tariff lookup (Section 301 / MFN / Trump 2025):**
- Section 301: `helpers/section301.js` + `routes/admin.router.js`
- MFN base rate: `helpers/hts-rates.js` (read from `data/hts-rates.json`); re-run `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` yearly
- Trump 2025: `helpers/trump2025.js` + `app_settings['trump_2025_tariffs']`

**Refresh the bundled HTS schedule:**
```bash
node scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js
```
Run once per year when USITC publishes a new revision. Commit the updated `data/hts-rates.json`.

**Add an email notification:**
1. `helpers/brevo.js` — Brevo transactional email wrapper
2. `services/notification.service.js` — in-app notification store
3. `helpers/mention.js` — @mention pipeline (auto-invoked from `addComment`)

| Change build or deploy | `cloudbuild.yaml`, `Dockerfile` | `config/env.js` (only if adding a new environment) |

| Add a Jest test | `tests/unit/<feature>.test.js` | Any source file (tests are read-only against the source) |

## 8. Data model

See `docs/architecture.md` for full Sequelize model details. Key entities:

| Entity | Key fields | Where defined | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `users` | `user_id`, `email`, `name`, `role`, `profile_photo` | `models/db/users.js` | `profile_photo` was added via safe migration (SYS-030 — see §14). Auto-provisioned on first Azure SSO login with `level: 'designer'`. |
| `Customer` | `customer_id`, `customer_name` | `models/db/Customer.js` | Confusingly used as the top-level namespace in `models/lib.model.js` |
| `Vendor` | `vendor_id`, `vendor_country`, `factory_id_fk`, `vendor_access` | `models/db/Vendor.js` | FK to `Factory`; `vendor_country` is the per-vendor country override. Rep impersonation resolves by `vendor_id` when the request body has `{ targetType: 'vendor' }`; visible factory-row impersonation resolves `Factory.id` when `targetType` is `factory` and can produce a vendor-scoped token with `email: null`. |
| `Factory` | `factory_id`, `factory_nickname`, `factory_country` | `models/db/Factory.js` | `factory_country` added May 2026 via safe migration; backfilled from most common `vendor_country` |
| `RFQVendor` | `RFQVendor_id`, `RFQitem_id_fk`, `vendor_id_fk`, `RFQVendor_amount`, `quote_date`, `req_status`, `requote_requested` | `models/db/RFQVendor.js` | One factory's quote on one RFQ item. `RFQVendor_id` is the `quote_id` the frontend sends. **`vendor_id_fk` stores `Factory.id`, not `Vendor.vendor_id`** (see §11). `requote_requested` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06 via safe migration) flags a stale price for refresh; mapped in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem`. |
| `RFQItem` | `rfqItem_id`, `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation`, `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, `Price Code` | `models/db/RFQItem.js` | `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` stores the raw 3-term duty breakdown (e.g. `"5.3+25+10"`). `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` (TEXT/JSON) maps each `*_price_sales` colId → `{duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at}`, snapshotting the cost inputs when a "Price for Sales" cell was populated; mapped both directions in `helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem`/`mapRFQItem`), mirroring `rfqItem_requested_price_cells`. |
| `app_settings` | `key`, `value` (VARCHAR, stores JSON) | `models/db/AppSetting.js` | Managed by `services/app-setting.service.js`; see §6 for key inventory |
| `user_notification` | `id`, `type`, `event`, `title`, `message`, `unread`, `user_id_fk` | `models/db/UserNotification.js` | Managed by `services/notification.service.js` |

**`app_settings` key inventory:**

| `ai-model` | OpenRouter model ID string | Admin → AI Settings |
| `section301_lookup` | JSON `{ "HTS": { list, rate, description } }` (~1 MB) | Admin → Section 301 CSV upload; legacy PDF endpoint still wired but UI-hidden |

## 9. Container inventory

| Container | Purpose | Managed by | Image tag pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| `designflow-backend` (this repo) | Core REST API | Google Cloud Run | `{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT}/{REPO}/{ENV}/backend:{BUILD_ID}-{SHA}` |

**Sibling services (separate repos — not documented here):**

---

## 10. What to ignore

Do not index or deeply analyze these paths:

```
node_modules/
coverage/
data/hts-rates.json   ← data, not code; ~1 MB; AI context budget wasted here
```

The `data/hts-rates.json` file is a generated snapshot. When you need to understand its shape, read the first 10 lines or the `helpers/hts-rates.js` comments — not the full file.

---

## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions

### `lib.router.js` and `lib.model.js` are giant files

**Looks like:** Poor modularization — 150+ endpoints in one file; `lib.model.js` is ~3000 lines.

**Actually:** Organic legacy growth. There is no formal split planned.

**Do not change because:** Splitting would touch every import and require a coordinated, carefully tested migration. The API surface is still in flux.

---

### `models/lib.model.js` — namespace is called `Customer` but contains everything

**Looks like:** Misnamed — the file handles vendors, factories, RFQ items, std products, art pieces, etc.

**Actually:** `Customer` was the first domain object. Everything else accreted around it.

**Do not change because:** The namespace name is internal. Renaming would touch every call-site for no user-facing benefit.

---

### Migrations live in `models/db.js`, not a migrations folder

**Looks like:** The wrong place for schema changes.

**Actually:** There is no `sequelize-cli`. The team uses inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` queries because they are idempotent and self-applying on every deploy.

**Pattern:**
```js
// Inside initialize() in models/db.js, after sequelize.sync():
sequelize.query(`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE`)
  .catch(err => console.warn('Migration <name> skipped:', err.message));
```

`${schema}` resolves to `"designflow".` in prod (or empty in test envs). Always add a matching assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Quote any mixed-case column identifier** (`"rfqItem_col"`), not just the table name. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case column is folded to lowercase by Postgres, producing an orphan column Sequelize can never query (this caused SYS-002 — see §14). Existing migrations already do this (e.g. `"rfqItem_duty_rate_equation"`, `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). All-lowercase names (e.g. `factory_country`, `profile_photo`) don't need quoting, but quoting them anyway is harmless and consistent.

---

### `pdf-parse` is lazy-required in `helpers/section301.js`

**Looks like:** Sloppy — package imported inside a function, not at module top.

**Actually:** `pdf-parse@1.1.1` has known load-time issues on certain Node versions; lazy-require defers the cost to the first PDF upload. v2.x is broken in Node — stay pinned to `1.1.1`.

**Do not change because:** Eager-requiring it can crash startup.

---

### `index.js` swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization`

**Looks like:** Odd first middleware that overwrites a standard header.

**Actually:** The BFF puts the OIDC service-to-service token in `Authorization` (so Cloud Run can read it) and the user JWT in `X-User-Authorization`. Cloud Run strips the OIDC token. This swap restores the user JWT to where `helpers/auth.js` expects it. **Without this swap, no route handler sees the user.**

**Do not change because:** Removing it breaks all authenticated routes.

---

### `cross-env NODE_ENV=...` in every start script

**Looks like:** Redundant `package.json` scripts for each environment.

**Actually:** Each script loads a different `.env.<env-name>` via `config/env.js`. Adding a new environment = new script + new `.env.<env-name>` file.

---

### `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` not in `--set-env-vars` in `cloudbuild.yaml`

**Looks like:** Missing configuration.

**Actually:** `--set-env-vars` in Cloud Build is a **full replacement** — anything not listed is wiped on next deploy. API keys and secrets belong in `--set-secrets` (Secret Manager refs), which persist independently. See §13 and §14.

---

### DeepSeek direct API alongside OpenRouter for AI classification

**Looks like:** Two separate AI client files (`helpers/openrouter.js` and `helpers/deepseek.js`) that do similar things — redundant.

**Actually:** DeepSeek is available via OpenRouter, but routing directly to `api.deepseek.com` is cheaper (no OpenRouter markup) and exposes the `thinking` parameter directly. The `reasoning_effort` field (`'none'|'high'|'max'`) maps to DeepSeek's `thinking: { type, budget_tokens }` internally; OpenRouter may not expose this parameter.

**How routing works:** `/api/openrouter/chat` checks the `api` field in the request body:
- `api = 'deepseek-direct'` → `helpers/deepseek.js`
- `api` omitted + model starts with `deepseek/` + `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set → auto-routes direct
- Everything else → `helpers/openrouter.js`

**`DEEPSEEK_DIRECT_MODELS`** in `routes/openrouter.router.js` is a hardcoded model list that mirrors OpenRouter's `/models` response shape. The frontend reads it at `GET /api/openrouter/models?api=deepseek-direct`. Includes `prompt_cache` pricing (~10% of input price) for accurate per-turn cost tracking.

**Do not change because:** DeepSeek's thinking-mode pricing (~$0.14/1M input, ~$0.014/1M cache hit) is meaningfully cheaper than going through OpenRouter for high-volume classifications.

---

### `RFQVendor.vendor_id_fk` holds a `Factory.id`, not a `Vendor.vendor_id`

**Looks like:** A foreign key to the `Vendor` table, given the column name.

**Actually:** It stores `Factory.id`. A `Factory` has many `Vendor` contact rows; the quote belongs to the factory, not an individual contact. `Customer.reqVendor` resolves the factory id from the vendor's `factory_id_fk` before writing it (`resolvedFactoryId = vendor_info.factory_id_fk`). The vendor JWT also carries `factory_id` (signed in `makeSingleToken` and the login path), so the two line up for the ownership guard below.

**Do not change because:** Renaming the column or "fixing" it to a real vendor id would break every quote lookup, the chosen-vendor logic, and `emailVendorsForFactory`.

---

### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open

**Looks like:** `helpers/quote-access.js` `vendorMayModifyQuote()` returns `true` in several cases instead of strictly denying — looks like a leaky check.

**Actually:** `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) is a sequential integer, so a malicious vendor could enumerate other factories' quotes and POST `updateVendor` / `keepPrice` against them (IDOR). The guard blocks the one real case — a `vendor` whose token `factory_id` differs from the quote's `vendor_id_fk` — and deliberately **fails open** for internal roles, vendors with no `factory_id` on their token (legacy records), and quote rows with no `vendor_id_fk`. The intent is to close the enumeration hole without ever locking out a legitimate user (notably the invite-an-individual-vendor-to-one-item flow, where the invited factory owns its own quote row).

**Do not change because:** Making it fail closed would block legacy vendors whose records were never linked to a factory, and `requestRequote` already hard-gates to internal roles separately. If you tighten it, backfill `factory_id` on all vendor records first.

---

### Trump 2025 `normalizeCountry` alias table is hand-curated

**Looks like:** Fragile string matching.

**Actually:** The alias table (`"PRC"` → `"china"`, `"Peoples Republic of China"` → `"china"`, etc.) is intentionally small and hand-curated. Admins should add factory countries under the name they use in their FOB Country master list to avoid alias gaps.

---

## 12. Environment and credentials

See `docs/configuration.md` for the full variable list. Key variables:

| Variable | Purpose | Where it lives |
|---|---|---|
| `DATABASE_URL` / `DB_*` | PostgreSQL connection | `.env.<env>` / Cloud Run secrets |
| `JWT_PUBLIC_KEY` / `JWT_PRIVATE_KEY` | RS256 signing | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
| `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` / `AZURE_TENANT_ID` | Azure JWKS verification | `.env.<env>` / `--set-env-vars` |
| `SCHEMA` | PostgreSQL schema name (default: `designflow`) | `.env.<env>` |
| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | AI proxy (OpenRouter) | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
| `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` | DeepSeek direct API | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` (`deepseek-api-key:latest`) |
| `DO_*` / `DIGITAL_OCEAN_*` | DigitalOcean Spaces | `.env.<env>` / secrets |
| `BREVO_API_KEY` | Transactional email | Secret Manager → `--set-secrets` |
| `PORT` | Express listen port (default 5000) | `.env.<env>` / `--set-env-vars` |
| `NODE_ENV` | Selects `.env.<NODE_ENV>` file | `package.json` start script |

**Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`.** That flag is a full replacement on every deploy — any value not listed is deleted. Secrets go in `--set-secrets` (Cloud Run Secret Manager references), which persist independently.

---

## 13. Deployment

See `docs/deployment.md` for the full pipeline. Summary:

- **CI/CD:** Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`) — no GitHub Actions
- **Build:** Single-stage Node `Dockerfile`; image pushed to Google Artifact Registry
- **Runtime:** Google Cloud Run (managed, serverless, stateless)
- **Deploy trigger:** Cloud Build trigger on push to the relevant branch
- **Rollback:** Cloud Run revision traffic split via `gcloud run services update-traffic`
- **Env vars:** Plain vars in `--set-env-vars` (full replacement per deploy); secrets in `--set-secrets` (persistent)
- **Branch policy:** `sandbox-albert` → sandbox Cloud Run service. Never merge directly to `develop` — PRs only.

---

## 14. Critical incident log

### 2026-05-29 — SYS-002: RFQ grid empty after unquoted `price_sales_snapshots` migration

**What happened:** The RFQ grid showed "no rows to show" with a "Something went wrong (SYS-002)" toast. Every RFQ load failed.

**Root cause:** The `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` migration in `models/db.js` was written with an **unquoted** identifier (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`). Postgres folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase, so it created `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots`. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so `RFQItem.findAll` queried the case-sensitive `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"` that never existed → `findAll` threw → `Customer.getAll` propagated the error to `controllers/main.controller.js` `findAll`, which returned `sendError(res, err, 'SYS-002')` → empty grid. Every other migration in the file already quoted its identifier; this was the lone exception. The shipped unit test had asserted the *unquoted* form, so CI green-lit the bug.

**Recovery:** Quoted the identifier (commit `a556d3f` → `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`) and added `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem" DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots"` (commit `476e453`) to remove the empty lowercase orphan column. Corrected `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` to require the quoted form and added guards: fail on the unquoted form, assert the orphan DROP exists, assert the correct mixed-case column is never dropped.

**Rule:** Inline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migrations in `models/db.js` **must quote any mixed-case identifier**. Sequelize quotes identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase, creating an orphan column Sequelize can never find. See §6 and §11.

---

### 2026 — SYS-030: `profile_photo` column missing in production, login crashes

**What happened:** `profile_photo` was added to the `users` Sequelize model (`models/db/users.js`) in commit `53cc531` without adding the matching `ALTER TABLE` migration in `models/db.js`. Every `sql.users.findOne()` in production failed with `column "profile_photo" does not exist`, crashing login.

**Root cause:** Sequelize `.sync()` does not alter existing tables. Adding a field to a model without a corresponding migration is a silent no-op in dev (if the dev DB already has the column) but fails in prod.

**Recovery:** Added the `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration to `models/db.js` and deployed.

**Rule:** Whenever you add a field to a Sequelize model in `models/db/*.js`, you **must** also add `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` in `models/db.js`. Add an assertion to `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

---

### 2026 — SSO 401: BFF OIDC audience mismatch (recurring — at least 3 times)

**What happened:** `POST /auth/azure` returned 401. Cloud Run logged: `The request was not authorized to invoke this service. The access token could not be verified.`

**Root cause:** The BFF uses `CORE_BACKEND_URL` as both the proxy target and the OIDC token `aud` claim. Cloud Run validates `aud` against the service's `.run.app` URL. Custom domains route traffic correctly via Cloud Run domain mapping **but do not work as OIDC audiences**. When `CORE_BACKEND_URL` was set to a custom domain, every OIDC token was rejected.

**Recovery:** Update `CORE_BACKEND_URL` (and `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, etc.) on the BFF Cloud Run service config and `designflow-bff/env/.env.*` to the `.run.app` service URL.

**Triage commands:**
```bash
# Check current BFF env vars on Cloud Run
gcloud run services describe designflow-bff --region=<REGION> --format='value(spec.template.spec.containers[0].env)'

# Update CORE_BACKEND_URL
gcloud run services update designflow-bff --region=<REGION> \
  --update-env-vars CORE_BACKEND_URL=https://<service-name>-<hash>-<region>.a.run.app
```

**Rule:** BFF `*_BACKEND_URL` values must **always** be `.run.app` service URLs, never custom domains. This applies to `CORE_BACKEND_URL`, `ITEM_MASTER_BACKEND_URL`, and any future sibling service URL. This has been the root cause of SSO outages at least 3 times.

---

### 2026 — Secrets wiped after deploy: `--set-env-vars` is a full replacement

**What happened:** A secret added manually to the Cloud Run service via the console disappeared after the next `cloudbuild.yaml` deploy.

**Root cause:** `--set-env-vars` in `gcloud run services update` replaces the entire set of plain env vars on the service. Any variable not in the flag is deleted.

**Recovery:** Move secrets to Secret Manager. Reference them in `cloudbuild.yaml` via `--set-secrets` (e.g. `--set-secrets=OPENROUTER_API_KEY=openrouter-api-key:latest`), which persists independently across deploys.

**Rule:** Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars`. All credentials go through Secret Manager + `--set-secrets`. Only non-secret config goes in `--set-env-vars`, and every variable that must survive a deploy must be listed there on every deploy.

---

### 2026-06-05 — Garbled Chinese in RFQ sub-grid headers: CP437 mojibake in `designflow_dev`

**What happened:** The RFQ detail/sub-grid column headers rendered as garbage (`Σ╗╖µá╝µèÑΣ╗╖ Quote Price`, `τ║╕τ«▒Θò┐σ║ª cm Carton length`) while hardcoded Chinese button labels in the same component (`detail-cell-renderer.component.html`, `rfq-button.component.html`) rendered correctly.

**Root cause:** The header text was **corrupt at rest in the database**, not a frontend or DB-connection bug. The Chinese was double-encoded: original UTF-8 bytes were decoded through **CP437** (the US Windows / `cmd.exe` OEM console codepage) and then re-saved as UTF-8. The `GridLayout` `rfq_detail` headers were just the *visible* symptom — a full scan (`scripts/scan-mojibake-everywhere.js`) showed the **entire `designflow_dev` schema is pervasively corrupted: ~35,000+ rows across 23+ text columns** (AuditLog, email_logs, RFQItem, RFQVendor, Factory, vendor, externalVendor, itemHeader, itemAttachment, licensingStatus, merchGroup, users, …). It affects all non-ASCII text — Chinese, accented Latin (`Décor`→`D├⌐cor`, `Cortázar`→`Cort├ízar`), symbols (`©`→`┬⌐`), smart quotes (`'`→`ΓÇÖ`), fullwidth punctuation, and NBSP. The corruption is **confined to `designflow_dev`**; the `designflow` and `designflow_sandbox` schemas scanned 100% clean (they hold the correct originals). This confirms the whole `designflow_dev` schema was hand-seeded through the bad CP437 console in one go. `SHOW server_encoding`/`client_encoding` were both already `UTF8`, so the connection was never the cause. The `designflow-data-syncing` service is NOT the culprit (it runs on Linux, which cannot produce CP437, and its `GridLayout` model is hardcoded to `schema: 'designflow_sandbox'`). The corruption came from a **one-time manual seed of `designflow_dev` run through a Windows console** (psql/pg_dump piped through `cmd`/PowerShell at codepage 437, or Chinese pasted into an interactive `cmd` psql session).

**How to recognize it:** Text shows box-drawing/Greek/accented glyphs (`Σ τ µ σ Φ ╗ ╝ ║ ▒ ░`). Reverse the double-encode with `iconv.encode(value, 'cp437').toString('utf8')` — if it yields clean CJK, this is the bug. (Use `cp437`, not `cp850` — cp850 fails on these rows.)

**Recovery:** Repaired the 12 visible `designflow_dev.GridLayout` header rows via the cp437 reverse-transform (scripts: `scripts/fix-mojibake-headers.*`, `scripts/scan-mojibake-everywhere.js`; backup JSON kept in `scripts/`). Added `client_encoding: 'UTF8'` to `dialectOptions` in `models/db.js` here and in `designflow-data-syncing/models/db.js` as defense-in-depth. **The remaining ~35k corrupted rows across the rest of `designflow_dev` were NOT auto-fixed** — a schema-wide reverse-transform needs per-value detection (skip already-clean rows, validate each result) + full backup; the safest fix is usually to re-seed `designflow_dev` server-side from the clean `designflow_sandbox` schema rather than transform in place.

**Rule:** **Never seed/copy `designflow_dev` (or any schema) by piping `pg_dump`/`psql` through a Windows console.** Multibyte text gets silently mangled by the OEM codepage. To (re)seed from the clean source in the same DB, copy server-side where text never touches a client codepage:
```sql
INSERT INTO designflow_dev."GridLayout"
SELECT * FROM designflow_sandbox."GridLayout"
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
```
If a dump/restore on Windows is unavoidable, force UTF-8 end to end: `chcp 65001`, `set PGCLIENTENCODING=UTF8`, and use `psql -f file.sql` (file I/O is UTF-8) rather than `<` redirection or pasting into the console.

---

## 15. Pending work

|---|---|---|
| pending | Notification trigger points — nothing calls `createNotification()` on business events | Add `NotificationService.createNotification()` calls in the backend at task assignment, status change, and approval events. Service API is in `services/notification.service.js`; endpoints are ready in `routes/notification.router.js`. |

file.content:
# Designflow Backend

Main API service for the Designflow PLM platform. Handles persistence, business integrations, and proxy endpoints for AI classification and CBP CROSS rulings.

**Active branch:** `sandbox-albert` — never merge or switch branches.

---

## Stack

- **Node.js** (CommonJS) + **Express 4.x**
- **PostgreSQL** + Sequelize ORM
- **JWT** (RS256) — verified per request via `helpers/auth.js`
- **DigitalOcean Spaces** (S3-compatible) — file storage for item images and customer logos
- **OpenRouter** — AI model proxy for HTS classification
- **Brevo** — transactional email
- **Jest 29** — 227 unit tests across 19 suites, hermetic (all external deps mocked), ~1 s

---

## Quick start

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env.sandbox-albert   # fill in real values
npm run start:sandbox-albert
```

```bash
npm test                               # run all unit tests
```

---

## Documentation

| Doc | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | Current internal API route inventory |
| [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md) | Module layout, all routes, auth flow, Azure SSO, Section 301, OpenRouter, notifications, impersonation, file storage |
| [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md) | Install, run, test, debug tips |
| [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md) | All env vars, Secret Manager vs env vars, app settings table |
| [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md) | Cloud Build pipeline, Cloud Run service, rollback, log retrieval |
| [`docs/mcp-integration.md`](docs/mcp-integration.md) | MCP + external API plan for AI clients, apps, data, and images |

file.content:
## Routes

### Health
- `GET /api/health` → `{ status: 'ok', message, timestamp }`

### Authentication
- `POST /auth/azure` — exchange Azure AD ID token for Designflow JWT; auto-provisions new users (unauthenticated)

### User
- `GET  /api/user/me` — current user profile `{ firstName, name, email, photoUrl }` (all roles)
- `POST /api/user/photo` — upload profile avatar (multipart `photo`, max 5 MB, all roles)

### Notifications
- `GET  /api/notifications` — unread notifications for current user (most recent 20, all roles)
- `GET  /api/notifications/count` — unread count for current user (all roles)
- `GET  /api/notifications/mentionable-users` — active users for @mention autocomplete (all roles)
- `POST /api/notifications/create` — create notification for a specific user (all roles)
- `PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read` — mark one notification read (all roles)
- `POST /api/notifications/read-all` — mark all notifications read (all roles)

## 2. Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section (§10).

---

## Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with:

- `AGENTS.md` (this file)
- `HANDOFF.md` **if it exists in the repo root** — it means work is mid-flight; read it before anything else.

Then load additional docs only when the task needs them. Do **not** ingest every `.md` file by default.

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Anything under `docs/` unless the task requires it |
| Modify app behavior or business logic (RFQ, vendor, product, tariff, notifications) | `AGENTS.md` (§3 structure, §6 decision tree, §7 task-to-file), `docs/architecture.md` if system design/data flow is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
| Add/change a DB column, model, migration, or external identifier | `AGENTS.md` (§6, §8), `docs/architecture.md`, `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` | `docs/deployment.md` unless rollout behavior changes |
| Add/change env vars, secrets, feature flags, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md` (§12), `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug, or package scripts | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, `package.json` | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
| Change build, Docker, Cloud Build/Run, release, or rollback | `AGENTS.md` (§13), `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `cloudbuild.yaml`, `Dockerfile` | Local-only dev docs unless needed |
| Look up an existing API route quickly | `docs/api-reference.md`, `docs/architecture.md` § Routes | Deep design docs unless behavior changes |
| MCP / external-API integration for AI clients | `docs/mcp-integration.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Unrelated route/migration docs |
| Investigate a bug or incident | `AGENTS.md` §14 (Critical incident log), docs for the affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present | Unrelated docs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, only the docs `HANDOFF.md` names | Docs outside the handoff scope |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless the task requires them |
| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected files under `docs/` | Source files except where needed to verify accuracy |

There are currently **no folder-level `README.md` files** in this repo; orientation lives in this file and `docs/`. If one is added, route to it from here.

---

## 3. Repository structure

content.text: Now the §11 idiosyncratic decision and the §14 incident. First the quirk (placed after the `RFQVendor.vendor_id_fk` quirk, which is thematically adjacent):

input.old_string: ### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open
input.new_string:
### Factory users live in the `vendor` table, not `users` — `/api/user/me` branches on role

**Looks like:** `/api/user/me` and `/api/user/photo` should just look the JWT's `user_id` up in the `users` table like every other user endpoint.

**Actually:** Two distinct identity tables back the one JWT shape. Internal/staff logins sign `user_id = users.id` (`controllers/auth.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js`). Factory logins sign `user_id = vendor.vendor_id` with `role: 'vendor'` (`models/lib.model.js` `makeSingleToken` / login path, `services/std.service.js`). So `services/user.service.js` `getProfile`/`updateProfilePhoto` take the **decoded token** and branch on `role`: `vendor` → `vendor` table by `vendor_id` (columns `vendor_name`/`vendor_lastname`/`vendor_email`/`vendor_profile_photo`); everyone else → `users` by `id`. The S3 avatar key is prefixed `vendor-<id>` vs `user-<id>` so the two independent id sequences never collide in the bucket.

**Why:** The original `/api/user/me` assumed every token's `user_id` pointed at `users`. For factory users it looked up a `vendor_id` in `users`, missed, and returned 404 — breaking factory login (see §14, 2026-06-08).

**Do not change because:** Removing the role branch re-breaks factory login. If you add another profile field, add it to **both** tables (or guard the read) — a vendor row has no `users` columns and vice-versa.

---

### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open

content.text: Now the §14 incident entry (newest first):

input.old_string:
## 14. Critical incident log

### 2026-05-29 — SYS-002: RFQ grid empty after unquoted `price_sales_snapshots` migration
input.new_string:
## 14. Critical incident log

### 2026-06-08 — Factory login 404 on `GET /api/user/me`

**What happened:** Logging in as a factory user (e.g. `jeromechen@homedecorbb.com`) produced a toast: `Me could not find the requested resource. HTTP 404; URL: …/api/core/api/user/me`. The route loads for every user on app start, so factory users were effectively blocked. Internal/staff users were unaffected.

**Root cause:** The new `/api/user/me` endpoint (`controllers/user.controller.js` → `services/user.service.js`) looked the JWT's `user_id` up in the `users` table for everyone. Factory JWTs carry `user_id = vendor.vendor_id` (signed in `models/lib.model.js` / `services/std.service.js`), and those users have no `users` row — so `findOne` returned `null` and the controller returned `404 User not found`. Not a routing or auth failure (role check passed; otherwise it would be 401).

**Recovery:** Made `getProfile`/`updateProfilePhoto` role-aware — `vendor` reads/writes the `vendor` table by `vendor_id`; everyone else uses `users`. Added `vendor.vendor_profile_photo` (model + safe migration in `models/db.js` + `db.migration.test.js` assertion) so vendor avatars have a home, and prefixed the S3 photo key `vendor-<id>` vs `user-<id>` to avoid cross-table id collisions. Added vendor-path unit tests in `tests/unit/user.profile.test.js`.

**Rule:** Any endpoint that resolves a user from the JWT must account for **two identity tables**: staff in `users` (token `user_id` = `users.id`) and factory users in `vendor` (token `user_id` = `vendor_id`, `role: 'vendor'`). Branch on `role`; never assume `user_id` indexes `users`. See §11.

---

### 2026-05-29 — SYS-002: RFQ grid empty after unquoted `price_sales_snapshots` migration

# Designflow Backend

Main API service for the Designflow PLM platform. Handles persistence, business integrations, and proxy endpoints for AI classification and CBP CROSS rulings.

**Active branch:** `sandbox-albert` — never merge or switch branches.

---

## Stack

- **Node.js** (CommonJS) + **Express 4.x**
- **PostgreSQL** + Sequelize ORM
- **JWT** (RS256) — verified per request via `helpers/auth.js`
- **DigitalOcean Spaces** (S3-compatible) — file storage for item images and customer logos
- **OpenRouter** — AI model proxy for HTS classification
- **Brevo** — transactional email
- **Jest 29** — 227 unit tests across 19 suites, hermetic (all external deps mocked), ~1 s

---

## Quick start

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env.sandbox-albert   # fill in real values
npm run start:sandbox-albert
```

```bash
npm test                               # run all unit tests
```

---

## Documentation

| Doc | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | Current internal API route inventory |
| [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md) | Module layout, all routes, auth flow, Azure SSO, Section 301, OpenRouter, notifications, impersonation, file storage |
| [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md) | Install, run, test, debug tips |
| [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md) | All env vars, Secret Manager vs env vars, app settings table |
| [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md) | Cloud Build pipeline, Cloud Run service, rollback, log retrieval |
| [`docs/mcp-integration.md`](docs/mcp-integration.md) | MCP + external API plan for AI clients, apps, data, and images |
