toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Development Guide

## Prerequisites

- **Node.js LTS** (match version in Dockerfile)
- A `.env.${NODE_ENV}` file — copy `.env.example` and fill in real values

## Install

```bash
npm install
```

## Run locally

```bash
npm run start:sandbox-albert   # NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert, nodemon, no test gate
```

Requires `.env.sandbox-albert` with all variables. The server logs the port, env file path, and key config on startup.

Other start scripts run `npm test` first and abort on failure:
```bash
npm start                       # NODE_ENV=localhost
npm run start:sandbox           # NODE_ENV=sandbox
npm run start:production        # NODE_ENV=production
```

## Run tests

```bash
npm test                                        # all suites (227 tests, ~1 s)
npm test -- --testPathPattern="section301"      # single suite
npx jest --watch                                # watch mode
```

Tests live in `tests/unit/`. All external dependencies (axios, pdf-parse, pg) are mocked — no real DB or HTTP calls needed. **Run before every commit.** There is no GitHub Actions CI and the Docker image does not run tests at build time. The `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts prefix `npm test &&`, so a failing test aborts container startup for those envs; `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` directly with **no test gate**, so you must run `npm test` yourself before pushing `sandbox-albert`.

There are also integration/e2e test scripts (`npm run test:e2e`, `npm run test:e2e:ui`, `npm run test:e2e:report`) using Playwright, but these are not part of the CI gate.

## Debug tips

### Auth failures (403 / 401)
Check the JWT payload in DevTools: `JSON.parse(atob(token.split('.')[1]))`. Verify `role` claim matches the roles in `authRole(...)` for the failing route. A 403 means the token is invalid or missing; a 401 means the role is not in the allowed list.

### Missing env vars at startup
`config/db.config.js` throws if any DB key is missing. `helpers/openrouter.js` `getKey()` throws synchronously if `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is absent. Both produce clear startup errors.

### pdf-parse errors on Section 301 upload
`pdf-parse` is lazy-required — it only loads on first `parsePdf()` call. If the package is missing or broken, the error surfaces on the first upload, not on startup.

### Context not populated
`contextMiddleware` runs before route handlers but after Express's built-in middleware. If `getContext()` returns null, the middleware is not mounted — check `index.js` middleware order.

### Inline migration column "does not exist" / grid returns SYS-002
Inline migrations in `models/db.js` must **quote mixed-case identifiers**. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column like `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is queried case-sensitively. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is folded to lowercase by Postgres, creating a column Sequelize can never find → `RFQItem.findAll` throws → `main.controller` returns SYS-002 and the RFQ grid shows "no rows to show". Always quote: `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`. Regression-guarded in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` (see AGENTS.md §14, SYS-002).

content.text:
Done — committed and pushed to `sandbox-albert` ([9787f23](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/commit/9787f23)).

## Documentation changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `AGENTS.md` | Added required **Documentation map: what to read for each task** section (routes tasks → specific docs, names `HANDOFF.md` as required reading when present, notes no folder-level READMEs exist). Aligned the Multi-model AI note wording. §8 data model: documented the two identity tables — `users` (staff) vs `vendor` (factory users), added `vendor_profile_photo`, fixed `users` PK to `id`. New §11 quirk: "Factory users live in `vendor`, not `users` — `/api/user/me` branches on role." New §14 incident: 2026-06-08 factory-login 404. New §7 nav row for the profile/avatar endpoint. §10 expanded to mirror ignore files. §15: added the `designflow_dev` re-seed pending item. Refreshed test count to 243/22. |
| `docs/architecture.md` | User routes now state the source table depends on JWT role, and where the avatar is stored per role. |
| `README.md` | Test count 227/19 → 243/22. |
| `docs/development.md` | Test count 227 → 243. |
| `CLAUDE.md` | No change — already short, defers to AGENTS.md, no duplication. |

## Ignore files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `.gitignore` | Added `scripts/mojibake-*.json` (generated DB dumps from the CP437 incident — kept out of git). |
| `.claudeignore` | Added `scripts/mojibake-*.json`. |
| `.cursorignore` | Added `scripts/mojibake-*.json` (kept identical to `.claudeignore`). |
| `.copilotignore` | Not created — Copilot is not used in this repo (noted in §10). |

## Handoff status

- `HANDOFF.md`: **absent**
- Reason: The vendor-profile fix is complete (code + 243 passing tests + docs, all committed). No work is mid-flight. Long-running open items (notification triggers, `designflow_dev` re-seed) are tracked in AGENTS.md §15, which is the right place for them — not session-handoff state.

## Verification

- Docs verified against actual repo: **yes** — every claim derived from inspected files (routers, controllers, services, models, `db.js` migrations, ignore files, `package.json` test run). Test counts come from an actual `npx jest` run (243 passed / 22 suites).
- Secrets added: **no** — §12 lists variable names only; no values written.
- Unknowns remaining: none introduced. Pre-existing documented open items (the ~35k unrepaired `designflow_dev` mojibake rows; notification trigger wiring) are flagged in §14/§15 with verification/next-action paths.

## Remaining documentation gaps

- `docs/api-reference.md` still lists `/api/user/me` tersely (one row); the role-dependent source-table nuance lives in `architecture.md` and AGENTS.md §11 instead — acceptable for a terse reference table, but could be cross-linked later.
- The untracked `scripts/*mojibake*.js` working tools are referenced in §14/§15 but not committed (left as local artifacts per scope). If they're meant to be reusable repo tooling, they'd warrant their own commit + a short `scripts/` note — flag for a future decision.
- This pass covered `designflow-backend` only. The sibling repos (`designflow-bff`, `designflow-item-master`, `designflow-data-sync`, frontend) have their own docs and were out of scope.

- **fix(user):** factory (vendor-role) login no longer 404s on `GET /api/user/me`. # CLAUDE.md — Claude Code notes for designflow-frontend

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first. It is the canonical guide for repo structure, custom-code boundaries, current architecture, incidents, deployment, and pending work. This file is only for Claude Code-specific operating notes.

## Memory and Branch

- User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Commit and push only to `sandbox-albert`
- Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally before editing.
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; use `develop` only for sync/PR base. Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then keep the GitHub PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated, or create it if missing.

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, asset, and legacy/demo template paths such as `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.angular/`, `.cache/`, `.yarn/`, `coverage/`, `src/assets/`, `src/static-data/`, and Vex demo pages.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `yarn`/`npm` scripts, run `ng`, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/` or `dist/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
yarn test
yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"
yarn test:watch
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. Add or update unit tests for every new or changed function/behavior. The Docker build also runs `yarn test`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- Use the AG Grid MCP docs before touching AG Grid code: set version `35.1.0`, framework `angular`; the repo uses AG Grid `35.2.1`.
- Do not use `.toPromise()` in new code; use `firstValueFrom()`.
- Do not add new `window['anything']` globals; use Angular DI.
- Do not add `aggFunc` to RFQ grid columns without reading `AGENTS.md`.
- For `mat-select`, keep options in a synchronous array, do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]`, and never add `(click)="select.open()"`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.

content.text:
Here's what I've established from the code:

- **Archive = a global flag** on `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` (`1` = archived, `null` = active). It's not per-vendor — archiving an item hides it from its active list for everyone (vendors and sourcing).
- **"Provided a price"** = an `RFQVendor` row exists with a quote (`RFQVendor_amount`, `quote_date`).
- **No scheduler exists** in the backend, and it runs on stateless Cloud Run (instances scale to zero), so a long-running `setInterval` won't reliably fire.

Before I build this, three decisions genuinely change the implementation and are yours to make:

questions.question: How should the auto-archive actually be triggered? (The backend has no scheduler today and runs on Cloud Run.)

options.label: Daily scheduled job
options.description: Add an authenticated endpoint (e.g. POST /api/admin/archive-stale-quotes) and a Cloud Scheduler job that hits it daily. True 'auto', reliable, runs once/day. Requires setting up a Cloud Scheduler job + OIDC auth (I'll provide the gcloud commands).

options.label: Lazy sweep on list load
options.description: Run the archive sweep when a vendor loads their RFQ item list. No new infra, but adds a write side-effect to a read and only runs when that vendor logs in.

options.label: Manual admin button
options.description: Expose the endpoint only; an admin clicks 'Archive stale quoted items' on demand. Simplest, but not automatic.

questions.question: 'Older than 3 months' should be measured from which date?

options.label: Quote date
options.description: 3+ months since the vendor provided the price (RFQVendor.quote_date). Archives items the vendor quoted and then nothing happened for 3 months. Most aligned with 'when they already provided prices'.

options.label: Item last-modified
options.description: 3+ months since RFQItem.rfqItem_date_modified — archives items with no activity of any kind for 3 months (as long as a price exists).

options.label: Item created date
options.description: 3+ months since the item was created, regardless of when it was quoted.

questions.question: Archiving is a global flag. When an item has several assigned vendors, when should it be archived?

options.label: Any quoted + old
options.description: Archive as soon as at least one vendor has quoted and the date threshold passed. Simple; matches a literal reading but may hide items where other vendors haven't quoted yet.

options.label: All vendors quoted + old
options.description: Archive only when every assigned vendor on the item has provided a price and the threshold passed. Safer — won't bury items still awaiting quotes.

lastPrompt: disregard #1, that was on develop branch, not sandbox-albert branch. 2) for all vendors auto-archive all items older than 3 months when they already provided prices for.

content.text: Key answer: archiving is **per-vendor**, not global. So I can't use the global `rfqItem_archive` flag — I need a per-vendor archive on the `RFQVendor` row (that vendor's quote/relationship to the item). Let me read the full vendor listing function to see exactly how items are filtered for vendors today.

Now I have the full picture. Plan, with your answers baked in (lazy sweep on vendor list load · measured from `quote_date` · **per-vendor** archive):

1. Add a per-vendor `RFQVendor_archived` column (the global `rfqItem_archive` can't be used — it'd hide items for everyone).
2. A small, unit-testable helper that archives a factory's stale quoted rows (priced + `quote_date` > 3 months old).
3. Call it lazily at the top of the vendor branch of `Customer.getAll` (the list-load path).
4. Switch the vendor list's archive/active filter to the per-vendor flag.

Let me confirm the require/header conventions in `lib.model.js` before editing.

# Designflow Backend

Main API service for the Designflow PLM platform. Handles persistence, business integrations, and proxy endpoints for AI classification and CBP CROSS rulings.

**Active branch:** `sandbox-albert` — never merge or switch branches.

---

## Stack

- **Node.js** (CommonJS) + **Express 4.x**
- **PostgreSQL** + Sequelize ORM
- **JWT** (RS256) — verified per request via `helpers/auth.js`
- **DigitalOcean Spaces** (S3-compatible) — file storage for item images and customer logos
- **OpenRouter** — AI model proxy for HTS classification
- **Brevo** — transactional email
- **Jest 29** — 243 unit tests across 22 suites, hermetic (all external deps mocked), ~1.5 s

---

## Quick start

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env.sandbox-albert   # fill in real values
npm run start:sandbox-albert
```

```bash
npm test                               # run all unit tests
```

---

## Documentation

| Doc | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | Current internal API route inventory |
| [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md) | Module layout, all routes, auth flow, Azure SSO, Section 301, OpenRouter, notifications, impersonation, file storage |
| [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md) | Install, run, test, debug tips |
| [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md) | All env vars, Secret Manager vs env vars, app settings table |
| [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md) | Cloud Build pipeline, Cloud Run service, rollback, log retrieval |
| [`docs/mcp-integration.md`](docs/mcp-integration.md) | MCP + external API plan for AI clients, apps, data, and images |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Development Guide

## Prerequisites

- **Node.js LTS** (match version in Dockerfile)
- A `.env.${NODE_ENV}` file — copy `.env.example` and fill in real values

## Install

```bash
npm install
```

## Run locally

```bash
npm run start:sandbox-albert   # NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert, nodemon, no test gate
```

Requires `.env.sandbox-albert` with all variables. The server logs the port, env file path, and key config on startup.

Other start scripts run `npm test` first and abort on failure:
```bash
npm start                       # NODE_ENV=localhost
npm run start:sandbox           # NODE_ENV=sandbox
npm run start:production        # NODE_ENV=production
```

## Run tests

```bash
npm test                                        # all suites (243 tests, ~1.5 s)
npm test -- --testPathPattern="section301"      # single suite
npx jest --watch                                # watch mode
```

Tests live in `tests/unit/`. All external dependencies (axios, pdf-parse, pg) are mocked — no real DB or HTTP calls needed. **Run before every commit.** There is no GitHub Actions CI and the Docker image does not run tests at build time. The `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts prefix `npm test &&`, so a failing test aborts container startup for those envs; `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` directly with **no test gate**, so you must run `npm test` yourself before pushing `sandbox-albert`.

There are also integration/e2e test scripts (`npm run test:e2e`, `npm run test:e2e:ui`, `npm run test:e2e:report`) using Playwright, but these are not part of the CI gate.

## Debug tips

### Auth failures (403 / 401)
Check the JWT payload in DevTools: `JSON.parse(atob(token.split('.')[1]))`. Verify `role` claim matches the roles in `authRole(...)` for the failing route. A 403 means the token is invalid or missing; a 401 means the role is not in the allowed list.

### Missing env vars at startup
`config/db.config.js` throws if any DB key is missing. `helpers/openrouter.js` `getKey()` throws synchronously if `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is absent. Both produce clear startup errors.

### pdf-parse errors on Section 301 upload
`pdf-parse` is lazy-required — it only loads on first `parsePdf()` call. If the package is missing or broken, the error surfaces on the first upload, not on startup.

### Context not populated
`contextMiddleware` runs before route handlers but after Express's built-in middleware. If `getContext()` returns null, the middleware is not mounted — check `index.js` middleware order.

### Inline migration column "does not exist" / grid returns SYS-002
Inline migrations in `models/db.js` must **quote mixed-case identifiers**. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column like `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is queried case-sensitively. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is folded to lowercase by Postgres, creating a column Sequelize can never find → `RFQItem.findAll` throws → `main.controller` returns SYS-002 and the RFQ grid shows "no rows to show". Always quote: `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`. Regression-guarded in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` (see AGENTS.md §14, SYS-002).

content.text: Now add the §11 decision documenting the two-archive-concepts split (anchoring before the quote-ownership-guard quirk):

input.old_string: ### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open
input.new_string:
### Two archive concepts: global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` vs per-vendor `RFQVendor_archived`

**Looks like:** Archiving an RFQ item should be one flag.

**Actually:** There are two, by role. Internal roles (sourcing/designer/etc.) archive the **item globally** via `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` (`1` = archived, `null` = active) — affects everyone. Vendors archive **per-vendor** via `RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row, so one factory clearing its stale items never hides them from Sourcing or other factories. In `Customer.getAll` the archive/active tab filter branches on role: vendors filter `$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$`; everyone else filters `$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$`. `Customer.archiveRFQItem` branches the same way (vendor → flip `RFQVendor_archived` on their row; internal → set `rfqItem_archive`).

**Auto-archive:** On every vendor list load, `Customer.getAll` calls `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes(sql, factoryId)`, which sets `RFQVendor_archived = true` for that factory's quote rows that were **priced** (`RFQVendor_amount` present) and quoted **>3 months ago** (`quote_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'`). Lazy (no scheduler — Cloud Run scales to zero), idempotent, and wrapped in try/catch so a sweep failure never breaks the listing. The threshold is compared DB-side (no JS `Date`).

**Do not change because:** Pointing the vendor archive filter back at the global flag re-introduces the cross-factory leak (one vendor's archive hiding an item from Sourcing). If you add an un-archive path for vendors, note the next list load re-archives anything still >3 months old by `quote_date` — that's intended.

---

### Vendor quote ownership guard fails open

**Actually:** There are two, by role. Internal roles (sourcing/designer/etc.) archive the **item globally** via `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` (`1` = archived, `null` = active) — affects everyone. Vendors archive **per-vendor** via `RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row, so one factory clearing its stale items never hides them from Sourcing or other factories. In `Customer.getAll` the archive/active tab filter branches on role: vendors filter `$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$`; everyone else filters `$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$`. `Customer.archiveRFQItem` branches the same way (vendor → flip `RFQVendor_archived` on their row; internal → set `rfqItem_archive`).

**Auto-archive:** On every vendor list load, `Customer.getAll` calls `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes(sql, factoryId)`, which sets `RFQVendor_archived = true` for that factory's quote rows that were **priced** (`RFQVendor_amount` present) and quoted **>3 months ago** (`quote_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'`). Lazy (no scheduler — Cloud Run scales to zero), idempotent, and wrapped in try/catch so a sweep failure never breaks the listing. The threshold is compared DB-side (no JS `Date`).

**Do not change because:** Pointing the vendor archive filter back at the global flag re-introduces the cross-factory leak (one vendor's archive hiding an item from Sourcing). If you add an un-archive path for vendors, note the next list load re-archives anything still >3 months old by `quote_date` — that's intended.

---

Brings the `sandbox-albert` work onto `develop`. Commits in this PR:

Lazy sweep runs on the vendor's list load (`Customer.getAll`, `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`); the vendor's archive/active tab and manual archive button both use the per-vendor flag. content.text:
Done — committed ([9623220](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/commit/9623220)), pushed to `sandbox-albert`, and PR [#34](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/pull/34) updated.

## What I built (per-vendor auto-archive)

Your answers drove the design: **lazy sweep on list load · measured from `quote_date` · per-vendor (not global)**.

The existing `rfqItem_archive` is a *global* flag, so it couldn't be used for per-vendor archiving. I added a per-vendor flag and routed the vendor experience through it:

| Piece | What it does |
|---|---|
| `RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived` (new BOOLEAN) | Per-vendor archive flag on the factory's own quote row — invisible to Sourcing/other factories. Model + safe migration (mixed-case quoted, per the SYS-002 rule) + assertions. |
| [helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js](designflow-backend/helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js) | `archiveStaleVendorQuotes(sql, factoryId)` — sets the flag on rows that are **priced** (`RFQVendor_amount` present) and **quoted >3 months ago** (`quote_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'`, compared DB-side). Idempotent. Unit-tested (6 tests incl. an injection guard). |
| `Customer.getAll` | Runs the sweep at the top of the vendor branch (lazy, wrapped in try/catch so it can't break the listing), and the vendor archive/active tab now filters `RFQVendor_archived` instead of the global flag. |
| `Customer.archiveRFQItem` | The manual archive button is now role-aware — vendors flip their own `RFQVendor_archived`; internal roles keep the global `rfqItem_archive`. (Caught this because leaving it would have made the manual button set the global flag while the tab read the per-vendor one.) |

**Verification:** full suite green — **253 tests / 23 suites** (+10).

**Behavior notes worth flagging:**
- **Trigger is lazy** (your choice): a vendor's stale items archive the next time *that vendor* loads their list. There's no daily job, so a vendor who never logs in won't have items swept. If you later want it to run for everyone regardless of login, that's the "daily Cloud Scheduler endpoint" option — say the word and I'll add it.
- **No un-archive for these:** if a vendor restores one, the next list load re-archives it while `quote_date` is still >3 months old. That's intended for an auto-archive feature; documented in AGENTS.md §11.
- This is **backend-only** — the vendor's existing archive tab surfaces the result; no frontend change was required.

Docs updated to match (AGENTS.md §8/§11/§7, test counts).

content.text: Confirmed: `IS NOT true` — matches both `false` and `NULL`. Correct. Now update the docs to reflect the new un-archive lifecycle. First, rewrite the §11 archive block (its old "next list load re-archives" note is now wrong):

input.old_string:
**Auto-archive:** On every vendor list load, `Customer.getAll` calls `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes(sql, factoryId)`, which sets `RFQVendor_archived = true` for that factory's quote rows that were **priced** (`RFQVendor_amount` present) and quoted **>3 months ago** (`quote_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'`). Lazy (no scheduler — Cloud Run scales to zero), idempotent, and wrapped in try/catch so a sweep failure never breaks the listing. The threshold is compared DB-side (no JS `Date`).

**Do not change because:** Pointing the vendor archive filter back at the global flag re-introduces the cross-factory leak (one vendor's archive hiding an item from Sourcing). If you add an un-archive path for vendors, note the next list load re-archives anything still >3 months old by `quote_date` — that's intended.
input.new_string:
**Auto-archive:** On every vendor list load, `Customer.getAll` calls `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes(sql, factoryId)`, which sets `RFQVendor_archived = true` for that factory's quote rows that were **priced** (`RFQVendor_amount` present), quoted **>3 months ago** (`quote_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'`), are not already archived, have **no pending re-quote** (`requote_requested IS NOT true`), and were **not manually un-archived** (`RFQVendor_archive_optout IS NOT true`). Lazy (no scheduler — Cloud Run scales to zero), idempotent, wrapped in try/catch so a failure never breaks the listing; threshold compared DB-side (no JS `Date`).

**Un-archive lifecycle (per vendor):**
- **Manual** — a vendor un-archiving (`Customer.archiveRFQItem`, `url=='archive'`) sets `RFQVendor_archived=false` **and `RFQVendor_archive_optout=true`**. The opt-out is sticky, so the next sweep won't re-archive it. A manual archive clears the opt-out.
- **Auto on re-request** — `Customer.requestRequote` (Sourcing asks for a fresh price) sets `requote_requested=true`, `RFQVendor_archived=false`, `RFQVendor_archive_optout=false`. So a re-request, for any reason, reactivates the item.
- **Cycle reset** — a fresh price (`reqVendor`/`updateVendor` path) and `keepPrice` both clear `RFQVendor_archived` + `RFQVendor_archive_optout` (and `keepPrice` refreshes `quote_date`), so the 3-month clock restarts and auto-archive can apply again later.

**Do not change because:** Pointing the vendor archive filter back at the global flag re-introduces the cross-factory leak (one vendor's archive hiding an item from Sourcing). Dropping the `requote_requested` / `RFQVendor_archive_optout` guards in the sweep would let it immediately undo a re-request or a manual un-archive on the next list load.

lines: **Actually:** There are two, by role. Internal roles (sourcing/designer/etc.) archive the **item globally** via `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` (`1` = archived, `null` = active) — affects everyone. Vendors archive **per-vendor** via `RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row, so one factory clearing its stale items never hides them from Sourcing or other factories. In `Customer.getAll` the archive/active tab filter branches on role: vendors filter `$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$`; everyone else filters `$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$`. `Customer.archiveRFQItem` branches the same way (vendor → flip `RFQVendor_archived` on their row; internal → set `rfqItem_archive`).

# Designflow Backend

Main API service for the Designflow PLM platform. Handles persistence, business integrations, and proxy endpoints for AI classification and CBP CROSS rulings.

**Active branch:** `sandbox-albert` — never merge or switch branches.

---

## Stack

- **Node.js** (CommonJS) + **Express 4.x**
- **PostgreSQL** + Sequelize ORM
- **JWT** (RS256) — verified per request via `helpers/auth.js`
- **DigitalOcean Spaces** (S3-compatible) — file storage for item images and customer logos
- **OpenRouter** — AI model proxy for HTS classification
- **Brevo** — transactional email
- **Jest 29** — 253 unit tests across 23 suites, hermetic (all external deps mocked), ~1.5 s

---

## Quick start

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env.sandbox-albert   # fill in real values
npm run start:sandbox-albert
```

```bash
npm test                               # run all unit tests
```

---

## Documentation

| Doc | Contents |
|-----|----------|
| [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | Current internal API route inventory |
| [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md) | Module layout, all routes, auth flow, Azure SSO, Section 301, OpenRouter, notifications, impersonation, file storage |
| [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md) | Install, run, test, debug tips |
| [`docs/configuration.md`](docs/configuration.md) | All env vars, Secret Manager vs env vars, app settings table |
| [`docs/deployment.md`](docs/deployment.md) | Cloud Build pipeline, Cloud Run service, rollback, log retrieval |
| [`docs/mcp-integration.md`](docs/mcp-integration.md) | MCP + external API plan for AI clients, apps, data, and images |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Development Guide

## Prerequisites

- **Node.js LTS** (match version in Dockerfile)
- A `.env.${NODE_ENV}` file — copy `.env.example` and fill in real values

## Install

```bash
npm install
```

## Run locally

```bash
npm run start:sandbox-albert   # NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert, nodemon, no test gate
```

Requires `.env.sandbox-albert` with all variables. The server logs the port, env file path, and key config on startup.

Other start scripts run `npm test` first and abort on failure:
```bash
npm start                       # NODE_ENV=localhost
npm run start:sandbox           # NODE_ENV=sandbox
npm run start:production        # NODE_ENV=production
```

## Run tests

```bash
npm test                                        # all suites (253 tests, ~1.5 s)
npm test -- --testPathPattern="section301"      # single suite
npx jest --watch                                # watch mode
```

Tests live in `tests/unit/`. All external dependencies (axios, pdf-parse, pg) are mocked — no real DB or HTTP calls needed. **Run before every commit.** There is no GitHub Actions CI and the Docker image does not run tests at build time. The `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts prefix `npm test &&`, so a failing test aborts container startup for those envs; `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` directly with **no test gate**, so you must run `npm test` yourself before pushing `sandbox-albert`.

There are also integration/e2e test scripts (`npm run test:e2e`, `npm run test:e2e:ui`, `npm run test:e2e:report`) using Playwright, but these are not part of the CI gate.

## Debug tips

### Auth failures (403 / 401)
Check the JWT payload in DevTools: `JSON.parse(atob(token.split('.')[1]))`. Verify `role` claim matches the roles in `authRole(...)` for the failing route. A 403 means the token is invalid or missing; a 401 means the role is not in the allowed list.

### Missing env vars at startup
`config/db.config.js` throws if any DB key is missing. `helpers/openrouter.js` `getKey()` throws synchronously if `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is absent. Both produce clear startup errors.

### pdf-parse errors on Section 301 upload
`pdf-parse` is lazy-required — it only loads on first `parsePdf()` call. If the package is missing or broken, the error surfaces on the first upload, not on startup.

### Context not populated
`contextMiddleware` runs before route handlers but after Express's built-in middleware. If `getContext()` returns null, the middleware is not mounted — check `index.js` middleware order.

### Inline migration column "does not exist" / grid returns SYS-002
Inline migrations in `models/db.js` must **quote mixed-case identifiers**. Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column like `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is queried case-sensitively. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots` is folded to lowercase by Postgres, creating a column Sequelize can never find → `RFQItem.findAll` throws → `main.controller` returns SYS-002 and the RFQ grid shows "no rows to show". Always quote: `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots" TEXT`. Regression-guarded in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js` (see AGENTS.md §14, SYS-002).

## Summary

Brings the `sandbox-albert` work onto `develop`. Commits in this PR:

- **feat(rfq):** per-vendor auto-archive of stale quoted items, with un-archive. When a vendor has provided a price and the quote is >3 months old (by `quote_date`), the item auto-archives for **that vendor only**, via `RFQVendor_archived` — never the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`, so it can't hide items from Sourcing or other factories. The vendor can **un-archive** their own item and it sticks (`RFQVendor_archive_optout` keeps the lazy sweep from re-archiving it). **Re-requesting a price** (`requestRequote`, for any reason) auto-un-archives. A fresh price or `keepPrice` resets the 3-month cycle. Sweep runs lazily on the vendor's list load (`Customer.getAll`, `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`). Migrations + model fields + tests.
- **fix(user):** factory (vendor-role) login no longer 404s on `GET /api/user/me`. Vendor JWTs carry `user_id = vendor.vendor_id`, looked up in the wrong table → 404. `getProfile`/`updateProfilePhoto` now branch on `role`; adds `vendor.vendor_profile_photo` + role-prefixed S3 keys.
- **fix(rfq):** bulk-offer xlsx template now opens on the **Offer Template** tab instead of the hidden Lookups sheet.
- **feat(rfq):** factory bulk offer upload — xlsx template generation + import (SYS-104/105).

- **docs:** API surface; AGENTS.md maintenance pass — Documentation map, two identity tables (`users` vs `vendor`), two archive concepts (global vs per-vendor) + un-archive lifecycle, incident log, ignore-file alignment, test counts.

## Testing

- `npm test -- --testPathPattern="archiveStaleQuotes|db.migration"` ✅ (per-vendor sweep, opt-out/re-request guards, migrations)
- `npm test -- --testPathPattern="user.profile"` ✅ (vendor profile path)
- `npm test -- --testPathPattern="rfqBulkImportTemplate"` ✅

content.text:
Done — committed ([661d356](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/commit/661d356)), pushed, and PR [#34](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-backend/pull/34) updated.

## What changed

Both new rules are in, built on the per-vendor archive with a sticky opt-out marker (`RFQVendor_archive_optout`):

**1. Vendor un-archive now sticks** — when a vendor un-archives their item ([`Customer.archiveRFQItem`](designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js)), it sets `RFQVendor_archived=false` **and** `RFQVendor_archive_optout=true`. The lazy sweep skips opted-out rows, so it won't silently re-archive on the next list load.

**2. Re-requesting a price auto-un-archives** — [`Customer.requestRequote`](designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js) (Sourcing asking for a fresh price, for any reason) now clears `RFQVendor_archived` and the opt-out alongside setting `requote_requested`. The sweep also skips any row with a pending `requote_requested`, so the re-request can't be undone while it's outstanding.

**Cycle reset** — a fresh price (`updateVendor`) and `keepPrice` both clear archive + opt-out (and `keepPrice` refreshes `quote_date`), so the 3-month clock restarts and auto-archive applies again later. Without that, a one-time un-archive would freeze the item active forever.

The sweep's guard is `IS NOT TRUE` on all three flags (`RFQVendor_archived`, `requote_requested`, `RFQVendor_archive_optout`) — I verified with the actual Postgres dialect that this matches both `false` and `NULL` rows.

**Verification:** full suite green — **257 tests / 23 suites** (+4). Migration follows the quoted-mixed-case rule; model fields + assertions added. Docs updated (AGENTS.md §8/§11 now document the full un-archive lifecycle).

One thing to flag: `requestRequote` is restricted to internal roles (`admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`) — that matches "**we** re-request a price." If a vendor self-initiated re-quote should also un-archive, that's a different path; tell me and I'll wire it.
Let me find every `.md` file and check which ones reference the areas I changed (vendor profile, archive, requote/keepPrice, RFQVendor) — not just the ones I've already been updating.

content.content:
=== ALL .md FILES ===
AGENTS.md
CLAUDE.md
README.md
docs/api-reference.md
docs/architecture.md
docs/configuration.md
docs/deployment.md
docs/development.md
docs/mcp-integration.md

=== REFERENCES TO AFFECTED AREAS ===
AGENTS.md:194:| Change vendor archive / auto-archive rules | `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`, `Customer.getAll` + `Customer.archiveRFQItem` in `models/lib.model.js`, `tests/unit/archiveStaleQuotes.test.js` | `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` / the internal-role archive path (vendor archive is per-`RFQVendor`, see §11) |
AGENTS.md:213:| `Vendor` | `vendor_id` (PK), `vendor_name`, `vendor_lastname`, `vendor_email`, `vendor_country`, `factory_id_fk`, `vendor_access`, `vendor_profile_photo` | `models/db/vendor.js` | The home table for **factory (vendor-role) users**. FK to `Factory`; `vendor_country` is the per-vendor country override. A vendor JWT's `user_id` claim is the `vendor_id` here, so `/api/user/me` and `/api/user/photo` branch on `role` to read/write this table (see §11). `vendor_profile_photo` (TEXT, added 2026-06-08 via safe migration) holds the avatar. Rep impersonation resolves by `vendor_id` when the request body has `{ targetType: 'vendor' }`; visible factory-row impersonation resolves `Factory.id` when `targetType` is `factory` and can produce a vendor-scoped token with `email: null`. |
AGENTS.md:215:| `RFQVendor` | `RFQVendor_id`, `RFQitem_id_fk`, `vendor_id_fk`, `RFQVendor_amount`, `quote_date`, `req_status`, `requote_requested`, `RFQVendor_archived` | `models/db/RFQVendor.js` | One factory's quote on one RFQ item. `RFQVendor_id` is the `quote_id` the frontend sends. **`vendor_id_fk` stores `Factory.id`, not `Vendor.vendor_id`** (see §11). `requote_requested` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06 via safe migration) flags a stale price for refresh; mapped in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem`. `RFQVendor_archived` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06 via safe migration) is the **per-vendor** archive flag — auto-set by the lazy sweep in `Customer.getAll` for priced quotes older than 3 months; governs the vendor's archive/active tab split. `RFQVendor_archive_optout` (BOOLEAN, added 2026-06) is the sticky "vendor manually un-archived this" marker that keeps the sweep from re-archiving it; cleared on re-quote/fresh-price/keepPrice (see §11). |
AGENTS.md:370:### Factory users live in the `vendor` table, not `users` — `/api/user/me` branches on role
AGENTS.md:372:**Looks like:** `/api/user/me` and `/api/user/photo` should just look the JWT's `user_id` up in the `users` table like every other user endpoint.
AGENTS.md:374:**Actually:** Two distinct identity tables back the one JWT shape. Internal/staff logins sign `user_id = users.id` (`controllers/auth.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js`). Factory logins sign `user_id = vendor.vendor_id` with `role: 'vendor'` (`models/lib.model.js` `makeSingleToken` / login path, `services/std.service.js`). So `services/user.service.js` `getProfile`/`updateProfilePhoto` take the **decoded token** and branch on `role`: `vendor` → `vendor` table by `vendor_id` (columns `vendor_name`/`vendor_lastname`/`vendor_email`/`vendor_profile_photo`); everyone else → `users` by `id`. The S3 avatar key is prefixed `vendor-<id>` vs `user-<id>` so the two independent id sequences never collide in the bucket.
AGENTS.md:376:**Why:** The original `/api/user/me` assumed every token's `user_id` pointed at `users`. For factory users it looked up a `vendor_id` in `users`, missed, and returned 404 — breaking factory login (see §14, 2026-06-08).
AGENTS.md:382:### Two archive concepts: global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` vs per-vendor `RFQVendor_archived`
AGENTS.md:386:**Actually:** There are two, by role. Internal roles (sourcing/designer/etc.) archive the **item globally** via `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive` (`1` = archived, `null` = active) — affects everyone. Vendors archive **per-vendor** via `RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row, so one factory clearing its stale items never hides them from Sourcing or other factories. In `Customer.getAll` the archive/active tab filter branches on role: vendors filter `$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$`; everyone else filters `$RFQItem.rfqItem_archive$`. `Customer.archiveRFQItem` branches the same way (vendor → flip `RFQVendor_archived` on their row; internal → set `rfqItem_archive`).
AGENTS.md:388:**Auto-archive:** On every vendor list load, `Customer.getAll` calls `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes(sql, factoryId)`, which sets `RFQVendor_archived = true` for that factory's quote rows that were **priced** (`RFQVendor_amount` present), quoted **>3 months ago** (`quote_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'`), are not already archived, have **no pending re-quote** (`requote_requested IS NOT true`), and were **not manually un-archived** (`RFQVendor_archive_optout IS NOT true`). Lazy (no scheduler — Cloud Run scales to zero), idempotent, wrapped in try/catch so a failure never breaks the listing; threshold compared DB-side (no JS `Date`).
AGENTS.md:391:- **Manual** — a vendor un-archiving (`Customer.archiveRFQItem`, `url=='archive'`) sets `RFQVendor_archived=false` **and `RFQVendor_archive_optout=true`**. The opt-out is sticky, so the next sweep won't re-archive it. A manual archive clears the opt-out.
AGENTS.md:392:- **Auto on re-request** — `Customer.requestRequote` (Sourcing asks for a fresh price) sets `requote_requested=true`, `RFQVendor_archived=false`, `RFQVendor_archive_optout=false`. So a re-request, for any reason, reactivates the item.
AGENTS.md:393:- **Cycle reset** — a fresh price (`reqVendor`/`updateVendor` path) and `keepPrice` both clear `RFQVendor_archived` + `RFQVendor_archive_optout` (and `keepPrice` refreshes `quote_date`), so the 3-month clock restarts and auto-archive can apply again later.
AGENTS.md:395:**Do not change because:** Pointing the vendor archive filter back at the global flag re-introduces the cross-factory leak (one vendor's archive hiding an item from Sourcing). Dropping the `requote_requested` / `RFQVendor_archive_optout` guards in the sweep would let it immediately undo a re-request or a manual un-archive on the next list load.
AGENTS.md:403:**Actually:** `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) is a sequential integer, so a malicious vendor could enumerate other factories' quotes and POST `updateVendor` / `keepPrice` against them (IDOR). The guard blocks the one real case — a `vendor` whose token `factory_id` differs from the quote's `vendor_id_fk` — and deliberately **fails open** for internal roles, vendors with no `factory_id` on their token (legacy records), and quote rows with no `vendor_id_fk`. The intent is to close the enumeration hole without ever locking out a legitimate user (notably the invite-an-individual-vendor-to-one-item flow, where the invited factory owns its own quote row).
AGENTS.md:405:**Do not change because:** Making it fail closed would block legacy vendors whose records were never linked to a factory, and `requestRequote` already hard-gates to internal roles separately. If you tighten it, backfill `factory_id` on all vendor records first.
AGENTS.md:454:### 2026-06-08 — Factory login 404 on `GET /api/user/me`
AGENTS.md:456:**What happened:** Logging in as a factory user (e.g. `jeromechen@homedecorbb.com`) produced a toast: `Me could not find the requested resource. HTTP 404; URL: …/api/core/api/user/me`. The route loads for every user on app start, so factory users were effectively blocked. Internal/staff users were unaffected.
AGENTS.md:458:**Root cause:** The new `/api/user/me` endpoint (`controllers/user.controller.js` → `services/user.service.js`) looked the JWT's `user_id` up in the `users` table for everyone. Factory JWTs carry `user_id = vendor.vendor_id` (signed in `models/lib.model.js` / `services/std.service.js`), and those users have no `users` row — so `findOne` returned `null` and the controller returned `404 User not found`. Not a routing or auth failure (role check passed; otherwise it would be 401).
AGENTS.md:460:**Recovery:** Made `getProfile`/`updateProfilePhoto` role-aware — `vendor` reads/writes the `vendor` table by `vendor_id`; everyone else uses `users`. Added `vendor.vendor_profile_photo` (model + safe migration in `models/db.js` + `db.migration.test.js` assertion) so vendor avatars have a home, and prefixed the S3 photo key `vendor-<id>` vs `user-<id>` to avoid cross-table id collisions. Added vendor-path unit tests in `tests/unit/user.profile.test.js`.
docs/api-reference.md:86:| `POST` | `/archiveRFQItem` | Archive RFQ item |
docs/api-reference.md:130:| `POST` | `/updateVendor` | Update vendor quote/data |
docs/api-reference.md:131:| `POST` | `/requestRequote` | Internal role requests refreshed quote |
docs/api-reference.md:132:| `POST` | `/keepPrice` | Vendor keeps existing price |
docs/api-reference.md:186:| `GET` | `/api/user/me` | Current user profile |
docs/api-reference.md:187:| `POST` | `/api/user/photo` | Upload profile photo |
docs/architecture.md:90:- `GET  /api/user/me` — current user profile `{ firstName, name, email, photoUrl }` (all roles). Source table depends on the JWT `role`: staff resolve from `users` by `id`; `vendor` (factory) users resolve from the `vendor` table by `vendor_id` (see AGENTS.md §11). Returns 404 only if no matching row exists.
docs/architecture.md:91:- `POST /api/user/photo` — upload profile avatar (multipart `photo`, max 5 MB, all roles). Stored on DO Spaces under `profile-photos/user-<id>` or `profile-photos/vendor-<id>` by role; URL persisted to `users.profile_photo` or `vendor.vendor_profile_photo`.
docs/architecture.md:136:- Vendor quotes (in `routes/lib.router.js`): `POST /updateVendor`, `/sendPriceReq`, `/addingVendor`. **Re-quote (stale price refresh):** `POST /requestRequote` (internal roles only — flags a factory's quote and emails the factory for a fresh price) and `POST /keepPrice` (vendor reconfirms the current price, clearing the flag). Both resolve the quote by `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) and run through the `vendorMayModifyQuote` ownership guard. See `models/lib.model.js` `Customer.requestRequote` / `Customer.keepPrice`.
docs/architecture.md:268:**`RFQVendor.requote_requested`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): true when Sourcing has asked a factory to refresh a stale quote. Set by `requestRequote`; cleared by `keepPrice`, by a new vendor price in `updateVendorData`. Surfaced to the frontend in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem` (drives the orange price cell + bilingual "Quoted <date>" tooltip in the RFQ detail grid). The migration quotes the identifier (`"requote_requested"`) per the SYS-002 rule even though it is all-lowercase.

AGENTS.md:403:**Actually:** `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) is a sequential integer, so a malicious vendor could enumerate other factories' quotes and POST `updateVendor` / `keepPrice` against them (IDOR). The guard blocks the one real case — a `vendor` whose token `factory_id` differs from the quote's `vendor_id_fk` — and deliberately **fails open** for internal roles, vendors with no `factory_id` on their token (legacy records), and quote rows with no `vendor_id_fk`. The intent is to close the enumeration hole without ever locking out a legitimate user (notably the invite-an-individual-vendor-to-one-item flow, where the invited factory owns its own quote row).
AGENTS.md:405:**Do not change because:** Making it fail closed would block legacy vendors whose records were never linked to a factory, and `requestRequote` already hard-gates to internal roles separately. If you tighten it, backfill `factory_id` on all vendor records first.
AGENTS.md:454:### 2026-06-08 — Factory login 404 on `GET /api/user/me`
AGENTS.md:456:**What happened:** Logging in as a factory user (e.g. `jeromechen@homedecorbb.com`) produced a toast: `Me could not find the requested resource. HTTP 404; URL: …/api/core/api/user/me`. The route loads for every user on app start, so factory users were effectively blocked. Internal/staff users were unaffected.
AGENTS.md:458:**Root cause:** The new `/api/user/me` endpoint (`controllers/user.controller.js` → `services/user.service.js`) looked the JWT's `user_id` up in the `users` table for everyone. Factory JWTs carry `user_id = vendor.vendor_id` (signed in `models/lib.model.js` / `services/std.service.js`), and those users have no `users` row — so `findOne` returned `null` and the controller returned `404 User not found`. Not a routing or auth failure (role check passed; otherwise it would be 401).
AGENTS.md:460:**Recovery:** Made `getProfile`/`updateProfilePhoto` role-aware — `vendor` reads/writes the `vendor` table by `vendor_id`; everyone else uses `users`. Added `vendor.vendor_profile_photo` (model + safe migration in `models/db.js` + `db.migration.test.js` assertion) so vendor avatars have a home, and prefixed the S3 photo key `vendor-<id>` vs `user-<id>` to avoid cross-table id collisions. Added vendor-path unit tests in `tests/unit/user.profile.test.js`.

docs/architecture.md:90:- `GET  /api/user/me` — current user profile `{ firstName, name, email, photoUrl }` (all roles). Source table depends on the JWT `role`: staff resolve from `users` by `id`; `vendor` (factory) users resolve from the `vendor` table by `vendor_id` (see AGENTS.md §11). Returns 404 only if no matching row exists.
docs/architecture.md:91:- `POST /api/user/photo` — upload profile avatar (multipart `photo`, max 5 MB, all roles). Stored on DO Spaces under `profile-photos/user-<id>` or `profile-photos/vendor-<id>` by role; URL persisted to `users.profile_photo` or `vendor.vendor_profile_photo`.
docs/architecture.md:136:- Vendor quotes (in `routes/lib.router.js`): `POST /updateVendor`, `/sendPriceReq`, `/addingVendor`. **Re-quote (stale price refresh):** `POST /requestRequote` (internal roles only — flags a factory's quote and emails the factory for a fresh price) and `POST /keepPrice` (vendor reconfirms the current price, clearing the flag). Both resolve the quote by `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) and run through the `vendorMayModifyQuote` ownership guard. See `models/lib.model.js` `Customer.requestRequote` / `Customer.keepPrice`.
docs/architecture.md:268:**`RFQVendor.requote_requested`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): true when Sourcing has asked a factory to refresh a stale quote. Set by `requestRequote`; cleared by `keepPrice`, by a new vendor price in `updateVendorData`. Surfaced to the frontend in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem` (drives the orange price cell + bilingual "Quoted <date>" tooltip in the RFQ detail grid). The migration quotes the identifier (`"requote_requested"`) per the SYS-002 rule even though it is all-lowercase.

file.content:
### File storage (mounted at root `/`)
- `POST /getPreSignedUrl` — generate S3 presigned PUT URL for direct browser upload (15 min expiry)
- `POST /setObjectPublic` — apply `public-read` ACL to an uploaded object; returns `{ publicUrl }` — called by frontend after upload to make the object publicly accessible
- `POST /saveTechLink/:id` — save a DO Spaces file URL to an item record
- `POST /deleteBucketObjects` — delete files from the bucket
- `GET  /getListOfBuckets`, `POST /getBucketObjects`, `PUT /setCORSForBucket` — bucket admin

### RFQ / product grid
- `POST /api/grid/getAllItem`, `getGridHeader`, `saveGridLayout`, `getCustomGridLayout`
- Vendor quotes (in `routes/lib.router.js`): `POST /updateVendor`, `/sendPriceReq`, `/addingVendor`. **Re-quote (stale price refresh):** `POST /requestRequote` (internal roles only — flags a factory's quote and emails the factory for a fresh price) and `POST /keepPrice` (vendor reconfirms the current price, clearing the flag). Both resolve the quote by `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) and run through the `vendorMayModifyQuote` ownership guard. See `models/lib.model.js` `Customer.requestRequote` / `Customer.keepPrice`.

### Other
- `/api/customers/**`, `/api/item-size/**`, `/api/item_depth/**`, `/api/comment/**`, `/api/role/**`, `/api/autofill/**`, `/std/**`

---

## Authentication

All protected routes use `authRole(roles[])` from `helpers/auth.js`:

1. Reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. For Cloud Run requests coming through the BFF, `index.js` first middleware swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` (the BFF puts the OIDC token in `authorization` for Cloud Run; the user JWT travels in `x-user-authorization` and is restored before any route handler runs)

261	
262	There is no `sequelize-cli` or migrations folder. `models/db.js` runs idempotent `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` / `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` / backfill `UPDATE` statements on every container start (see `deployment.md` → Startup migrations). Each model field in `models/db/*.js` must have a matching statement here, plus an assertion in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.
263	
264	**Quote mixed-case identifiers.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column is queried case-sensitively (e.g. `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_foo` is folded to lowercase `rfqitem_foo` by Postgres and never matches Sequelize's quoted `SELECT` — every `RFQItem.findAll` then throws and the RFQ grid returns SYS-002. This was a live incident; see AGENTS.md §14.

file.content:
**Endpoints:** see Routes → Admin above. The compact `countries` endpoint is intentionally readable by every RFQ-viewing role (designer, sales, production, vendor included) because every quote row needs it; the heavier `listAll` and the update are gated to admin / sourcing_manager.

---

## Inline migrations (`models/db.js`)

There is no `sequelize-cli` or migrations folder. `models/db.js` runs idempotent `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` / `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` / backfill `UPDATE` statements on every container start (see `deployment.md` → Startup migrations). Each model field in `models/db/*.js` must have a matching statement here, plus an assertion in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`.

**Quote mixed-case identifiers.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column is queried case-sensitively (e.g. `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_foo` is folded to lowercase `rfqitem_foo` by Postgres and never matches Sequelize's quoted `SELECT` — every `RFQItem.findAll` then throws and the RFQ grid returns SYS-002. This was a live incident; see AGENTS.md §14.

**`RFQItem.rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`** (`TEXT`, nullable): per-row JSON map of `price_sales` colId → `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }`, snapshotting the cost inputs at the moment each "Price for Sales" cell was last populated (surfaced as a cell tooltip). Mapped both directions in `helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem` / `mapRFQItem`).

**`RFQVendor.requote_requested`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): true when Sourcing has asked a factory to refresh a stale quote. Set by `requestRequote`; cleared by `keepPrice`, by a new vendor price in `updateVendorData`. Surfaced to the frontend in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem` (drives the orange price cell + bilingual "Quoted <date>" tooltip in the RFQ detail grid). The migration quotes the identifier (`"requote_requested"`) per the SYS-002 rule even though it is all-lowercase.

---

## OpenRouter proxy

`helpers/openrouter.js`:
- `listUserModels()` — 12 s timeout
- `chatCompletion({ model, messages, max_tokens, temperature })` — 60 s timeout

- **Archive:** `POST /archiveRFQItem` archives/un-archives an item. For internal roles it toggles the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`; for vendors it toggles a **per-vendor** `RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row (so it never hides the item from Sourcing or other factories). On a vendor's list load, `Customer.getAll` auto-archives that factory's priced quotes older than 3 months (`helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`). A vendor un-archive is sticky (`RFQVendor_archive_optout`); `requestRequote`, a fresh price, and `keepPrice` auto-un-archive / reset the cycle. See AGENTS.md §11.

**`RFQVendor.requote_requested`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): true when Sourcing has asked a factory to refresh a stale quote. **`RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): per-vendor archive flag on a factory's own quote row. Drives the vendor's archive/active tab split in `Customer.getAll` (vendors filter `$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$`; internal roles still use the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`). Auto-set by the lazy sweep `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes()` for priced quotes whose `quote_date` is >3 months old. Mixed-case identifier — migration quotes it (`"RFQVendor_archived"`).

**`RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archive_optout`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): sticky "vendor manually un-archived this" marker. The sweep skips rows where this (or `requote_requested`) `IS NOT true`, so a manual un-archive isn't undone on the next list load. Cleared on `requestRequote`, a fresh vendor price (`updateVendor`), and `keepPrice`, so the 3-month auto-archive cycle restarts. See AGENTS.md §11 for the full lifecycle.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Deployment

## Pipeline overview

```
git push → Cloud Build trigger
  → Step 1: docker build (node:lts-alpine → npm install → CMD yarn start:${NODE_ENV})
  → Step 2: docker push → Artifact Registry
  → Step 3: gcloud run deploy → Cloud Run
  → container start: models/db.js runs inline DB migrations
```

No GitHub Actions. CI/CD is handled entirely by Google Cloud Build (`cloudbuild.yaml`).

## Dockerfile

- Base: `node:lts-alpine`
- Installs ImageMagick (for image processing endpoints)
- Copies source, runs `yarn install` (includes devDependencies for `cross-env` and `nodemon`)
- Exposes port 5000
- CMD: `yarn start:$NODE_ENV` — uses the start script matching the injected `NODE_ENV`

The backend Docker image does **not** run tests at build time (unlike the frontend), and there is no separate GitHub Actions CI. Tests gate startup only for the `start:sandbox` and `start:production` scripts (which prefix `npm test &&`); `start:sandbox-albert` runs `nodemon` with no test gate. Run `npm test` locally before pushing. See [`development.md`](development.md).

## Startup migrations

There is no separate migration job. On **every container start**, `models/db.js` (loaded via `index.js`) runs a sequence of idempotent inline migrations against the database — `ALTER TABLE "RFQItem"/"Factory"/"users"/"customers" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS app_settings`, and a `Factory.factory_country` backfill `UPDATE`. A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.

**Mixed-case column identifiers MUST be double-quoted** in these statements. Sequelize quotes all identifiers; Postgres folds unquoted ones to lowercase. An unquoted `ADD COLUMN` created an orphan `rfqitem_price_sales_snapshots` that broke the RFQ grid (SYS-002, fixed in `a556d3f`). See AGENTS.md §14.

## Image tag format

```
{REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/{PROJECT_ID}/{ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/{APP_ENV}/{APP_NAME}:{BUILD_ID}-{COMMIT_SHA}
```

## Cloud Run service

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Service name | `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` (sandbox-albert env) |
| GCP project | `lithe-breaker-323913` |
| Region | `us-central1` |
| Auth | `--no-allow-unauthenticated` (requests authenticated by BFF with OIDC token) |
| VPC connector | configured via `_VPC_CONNECTOR` trigger variable |

Secrets are injected at deploy time via `--set-secrets`; non-secret config via `--set-env-vars`. See [`configuration.md`](configuration.md) for the full variable list.

## Triggering a build

The Cloud Build trigger `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` is configured (in the Google Cloud Console) to fire on push to the `sandbox-albert` branch and auto-deploys the new revision. (The Cloud Run service for this env happens to share the same name.) Each environment has its own trigger with its own substitution variables.

## Environments

| Environment | Branch | Cloud Run service | Domain | Purpose |
|-------------|--------|-------------------|--------|---------|
| `sandbox-albert` | `sandbox-albert` | `popcre-albert-backend-sandbox` | `api.core.alsand.designflow.app` | Albert's personal dev/test |
| `sandbox` | `develop` | `popcre-core-sandbox` | `api.core.sandbox.designflow.app` | Shared staging |
| `production` | `main` | `popcre-core-prod` | `api.core.designflow.app` | Live production |

**Note on domain names vs OIDC:** The domain column is for display/routing only. BFF services always call backends using the `.run.app` service URL as both the proxy target and OIDC token audience — not the custom domain. See the BFF repo `AGENTS.md` for the full explanation.

## Rollback

```bash
# List revisions
gcloud run revisions list \
  --service=popcre-albert-backend-sandbox \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --project=lithe-breaker-323913

# Shift 100% traffic to a prior revision
gcloud run services update-traffic popcre-albert-backend-sandbox \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 \
  --to-revisions=<REVISION_NAME>=100
```

## Viewing build logs

Cloud Build uses `logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`. Retrieve logs via Cloud Logging:

```bash
# Find recent build IDs
gcloud logging read "resource.type=build" \
  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 --limit=5 --format=json

# Stream full log for a specific build
gcloud logging read "resource.labels.build_id=<BUILD_ID>" \
  --project=lithe-breaker-323913 --limit=200 \
  --format="value(textPayload)"
```

## Service account requirements

The Cloud Run service account `deployer@lithe-breaker-323913.iam.gserviceaccount.com` must have `roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor` on every secret listed in `--set-secrets`. Granting access to a new secret is part of the [secret creation procedure](configuration.md#adding-a-new-secret).

A schema change ships by adding an `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` statement to `models/db.js`; the new column is only realized in an environment once the new revision boots.

I audited all 9 `.md` files in the repo against this session's changes (vendor profile fix + per-vendor archive/un-archive):

|---|---|
| `docs/architecture.md` | **Updated** — added an Archive route note (per-vendor vs global, lazy sweep, sticky un-archive, auto-un-archive on re-request) and two data-model entries for `RFQVendor_archived` / `RFQVendor_archive_optout`. |
| `docs/api-reference.md` | **Updated** — clarified the `/archiveRFQItem`, `/requestRequote`, `/api/user/me`, and `/api/user/photo` rows (were understated/generic). |
| `docs/deployment.md` | **Updated** — added `"vendor"` and `"RFQVendor"` to the enumerated list of migrated tables in the Startup migrations section. |
| `AGENTS.md` | Already current — kept in sync during the feature work (§8 data model, §11 lifecycle, §7 nav, §14 incident, test counts). |
| `README.md` | Already current — test count `257/23`. |
| `docs/development.md` | Already current — test count `257`. |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Not affected — Claude-specific notes only. |
| `docs/configuration.md` | Not affected — no env/config changes (only DB columns). |
| `docs/mcp-integration.md` | Not affected — doesn't reference these endpoints/tables. |

No secrets added, and the only untracked files left are the (intentionally excluded) mojibake working scripts.
